1/** @file
2 * @brief MAVLink comm protocol generated from common.xml
3 * @see http://mavlink.org
4 */
5#pragma once
6#ifndef MAVLINK_COMMON_H
7#define MAVLINK_COMMON_H
8
9#ifndef MAVLINK_H
10 #error Wrong include order: MAVLINK_COMMON.H MUST NOT BE DIRECTLY USED. Include mavlink.h from the same directory instead or set ALL AND EVERY defines from MAVLINK.H manually accordingly, including the #define MAVLINK_H call.
11#endif

31#define MAVLINK_ENABLED_COMMON
32
33// ENUM DEFINITIONS
34
35
36/** @brief Flags to report failure cases over the high latency telemetry. */
37#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_HL_FAILURE_FLAG
38#define HAVE_ENUM_HL_FAILURE_FLAG
39typedef enum HL_FAILURE_FLAG
40{
41 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_GPS=1, /* GPS failure. | */
42 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_DIFFERENTIAL_PRESSURE=2, /* Differential pressure sensor failure. | */
43 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_ABSOLUTE_PRESSURE=4, /* Absolute pressure sensor failure. | */
44 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_3D_ACCEL=8, /* Accelerometer sensor failure. | */
45 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_3D_GYRO=16, /* Gyroscope sensor failure. | */
46 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_3D_MAG=32, /* Magnetometer sensor failure. | */
47 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_TERRAIN=64, /* Terrain subsystem failure. | */
48 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_BATTERY=128, /* Battery failure/critical low battery. | */
49 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_RC_RECEIVER=256, /* RC receiver failure/no RC connection. | */
50 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_OFFBOARD_LINK=512, /* Offboard link failure. | */
51 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_ENGINE=1024, /* Engine failure. | */
52 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_GEOFENCE=2048, /* Geofence violation. | */
53 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_ESTIMATOR=4096, /* Estimator failure, for example measurement rejection or large variances. | */
54 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_MISSION=8192, /* Mission failure. | */
55 HL_FAILURE_FLAG_ENUM_END=8193, /* | */
56} HL_FAILURE_FLAG;
57#endif
58
59/** @brief Actions that may be specified in MAV_CMD_OVERRIDE_GOTO to override mission execution. */
60#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GOTO
61#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GOTO
62typedef enum MAV_GOTO
63{
64 MAV_GOTO_DO_HOLD=0, /* Hold at the current position. | */
65 MAV_GOTO_DO_CONTINUE=1, /* Continue with the next item in mission execution. | */
66 MAV_GOTO_HOLD_AT_CURRENT_POSITION=2, /* Hold at the current position of the system | */
67 MAV_GOTO_HOLD_AT_SPECIFIED_POSITION=3, /* Hold at the position specified in the parameters of the DO_HOLD action | */
68 MAV_GOTO_ENUM_END=4, /* | */
69} MAV_GOTO;
70#endif
71
72/** @brief Predefined OR-combined MAV_MODE_FLAG values. These can simplify using the flags when setting modes. Note that manual input is enabled in all modes as a safety override. */
73#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE
74#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MODE
75typedef enum MAV_MODE
76{
77 MAV_MODE_PREFLIGHT=0, /* System is not ready to fly, booting, calibrating, etc. No flag is set. | */
78 MAV_MODE_MANUAL_DISARMED=64, /* System is allowed to be active, under manual (RC) control, no stabilization (MAV_MODE_FLAG_MANUAL_INPUT_ENABLED) | */
79 MAV_MODE_TEST_DISARMED=66, /* UNDEFINED mode. This solely depends on the autopilot - use with caution, intended for developers only. (MAV_MODE_FLAG_MANUAL_INPUT_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_TEST_ENABLED). | */
80 MAV_MODE_STABILIZE_DISARMED=80, /* System is allowed to be active, under assisted RC control (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_STABILIZE_ENABLED) | */
81 MAV_MODE_GUIDED_DISARMED=88, /* System is allowed to be active, under autonomous control, manual setpoint (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_STABILIZE_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_GUIDED_ENABLED) | */
82 MAV_MODE_AUTO_DISARMED=92, /* System is allowed to be active, under autonomous control and navigation (the trajectory is decided onboard and not pre-programmed by waypoints). (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_STABILIZE_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_GUIDED_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_AUTO_ENABLED). | */
83 MAV_MODE_MANUAL_ARMED=192, /* System is allowed to be active, under manual (RC) control, no stabilization (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_MANUAL_INPUT_ENABLED) | */
84 MAV_MODE_TEST_ARMED=194, /* UNDEFINED mode. This solely depends on the autopilot - use with caution, intended for developers only (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_MANUAL_INPUT_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_TEST_ENABLED) | */
85 MAV_MODE_STABILIZE_ARMED=208, /* System is allowed to be active, under assisted RC control (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_MANUAL_INPUT_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_STABILIZE_ENABLED) | */
86 MAV_MODE_GUIDED_ARMED=216, /* System is allowed to be active, under autonomous control, manual setpoint (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_MANUAL_INPUT_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_STABILIZE_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_GUIDED_ENABLED) | */
87 MAV_MODE_AUTO_ARMED=220, /* System is allowed to be active, under autonomous control and navigation (the trajectory is decided onboard and not pre-programmed by waypoints). (MAV_MODE_FLAG_SAFETY_ARMED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_MANUAL_INPUT_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_STABILIZE_ENABLED, MAV_MODE_FLAG_GUIDED_ENABLED,MAV_MODE_FLAG_AUTO_ENABLED). | */
88 MAV_MODE_ENUM_END=221, /* | */
89} MAV_MODE;
90#endif
91
92/** @brief These encode the sensors whose status is sent as part of the SYS_STATUS message. */
93#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR
94#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR
95typedef enum MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR
96{
97 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_3D_GYRO=1, /* 0x01 3D gyro | */
98 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_3D_ACCEL=2, /* 0x02 3D accelerometer | */
99 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_3D_MAG=4, /* 0x04 3D magnetometer | */
100 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_ABSOLUTE_PRESSURE=8, /* 0x08 absolute pressure | */
101 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_DIFFERENTIAL_PRESSURE=16, /* 0x10 differential pressure | */
102 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_GPS=32, /* 0x20 GPS | */
103 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_OPTICAL_FLOW=64, /* 0x40 optical flow | */
104 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_VISION_POSITION=128, /* 0x80 computer vision position | */
105 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_LASER_POSITION=256, /* 0x100 laser based position | */
106 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTERNAL_GROUND_TRUTH=512, /* 0x200 external ground truth (Vicon or Leica) | */
107 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_ANGULAR_RATE_CONTROL=1024, /* 0x400 3D angular rate control | */
108 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_ATTITUDE_STABILIZATION=2048, /* 0x800 attitude stabilization | */
109 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_YAW_POSITION=4096, /* 0x1000 yaw position | */
110 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_Z_ALTITUDE_CONTROL=8192, /* 0x2000 z/altitude control | */
111 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_XY_POSITION_CONTROL=16384, /* 0x4000 x/y position control | */
112 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_MOTOR_OUTPUTS=32768, /* 0x8000 motor outputs / control | */
113 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_RC_RECEIVER=65536, /* 0x10000 RC receiver | */
114 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_3D_GYRO2=131072, /* 0x20000 2nd 3D gyro | */
115 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_3D_ACCEL2=262144, /* 0x40000 2nd 3D accelerometer | */
116 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_3D_MAG2=524288, /* 0x80000 2nd 3D magnetometer | */
117 MAV_SYS_STATUS_GEOFENCE=1048576, /* 0x100000 geofence | */
118 MAV_SYS_STATUS_AHRS=2097152, /* 0x200000 AHRS subsystem health | */
119 MAV_SYS_STATUS_TERRAIN=4194304, /* 0x400000 Terrain subsystem health | */
120 MAV_SYS_STATUS_REVERSE_MOTOR=8388608, /* 0x800000 Motors are reversed | */
121 MAV_SYS_STATUS_LOGGING=16777216, /* 0x1000000 Logging | */
122 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_BATTERY=33554432, /* 0x2000000 Battery | */
123 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_PROXIMITY=67108864, /* 0x4000000 Proximity | */
124 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_SATCOM=134217728, /* 0x8000000 Satellite Communication | */
125 MAV_SYS_STATUS_PREARM_CHECK=268435456, /* 0x10000000 pre-arm check status. Always healthy when armed | */
126 MAV_SYS_STATUS_OBSTACLE_AVOIDANCE=536870912, /* 0x20000000 Avoidance/collision prevention | */
127 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_PROPULSION=1073741824, /* 0x40000000 propulsion (actuator, esc, motor or propellor) | */
128 MAV_SYS_STATUS_EXTENSION_USED=2147483648, /* 0x80000000 Extended bit-field are used for further sensor status bits (needs to be set in onboard_control_sensors_present only) | */
129 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_ENUM_END=2147483649, /* | */
130} MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR;
131#endif
132
133/** @brief These encode the sensors whose status is sent as part of the SYS_STATUS message in the extended fields. */
134#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED
135#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED
136typedef enum MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED
137{
138 MAV_SYS_STATUS_RECOVERY_SYSTEM=1, /* 0x01 Recovery system (parachute, balloon, retracts etc) | */
139 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_LEAK=2, /* 0x02 Leak detection | */
140 MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED_ENUM_END=3, /* | */
141} MAV_SYS_STATUS_SENSOR_EXTENDED;
142#endif
143
144/** @brief Coordinate frames used by MAVLink. Not all frames are supported by all commands, messages, or vehicles.
145
146 Global frames use the following naming conventions:
147 - "GLOBAL": Global coordinate frame with WGS84 latitude/longitude and altitude positive over mean sea level (MSL) by default.
148 The following modifiers may be used with "GLOBAL":
149 - "RELATIVE_ALT": Altitude is relative to the vehicle home position rather than MSL.
150 - "TERRAIN_ALT": Altitude is relative to ground level rather than MSL.
151 - "INT": Latitude/longitude (in degrees) are scaled by multiplying by 1E7.
152
153 Local frames use the following naming conventions:
154 - "LOCAL": Origin of local frame is fixed relative to earth. Unless otherwise specified this origin is the origin of the vehicle position-estimator ("EKF").
155 - "BODY": Origin of local frame travels with the vehicle. NOTE, "BODY" does NOT indicate alignment of frame axis with vehicle attitude.
156 - "OFFSET": Deprecated synonym for "BODY" (origin travels with the vehicle). Not to be used for new frames.
157
158 Some deprecated frames do not follow these conventions (e.g. MAV_FRAME_BODY_NED and MAV_FRAME_BODY_OFFSET_NED).
159 */
160#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FRAME
161#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FRAME
162typedef enum MAV_FRAME
163{
164 MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL=0, /* Global (WGS84) coordinate frame + altitude relative to mean sea level (MSL). | */
165 MAV_FRAME_LOCAL_NED=1, /* NED local tangent frame (x: North, y: East, z: Down) with origin fixed relative to earth. | */
166 MAV_FRAME_MISSION=2, /* NOT a coordinate frame, indicates a mission command. | */
167 MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL_RELATIVE_ALT=3, /*
168 Global (WGS84) coordinate frame + altitude relative to the home position.
169 | */
170 MAV_FRAME_LOCAL_ENU=4, /* ENU local tangent frame (x: East, y: North, z: Up) with origin fixed relative to earth. | */
171 MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL_INT=5, /* Global (WGS84) coordinate frame (scaled) + altitude relative to mean sea level (MSL). | */
172 MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL_RELATIVE_ALT_INT=6, /* Global (WGS84) coordinate frame (scaled) + altitude relative to the home position. | */
173 MAV_FRAME_LOCAL_OFFSET_NED=7, /* NED local tangent frame (x: North, y: East, z: Down) with origin that travels with the vehicle. | */
174 MAV_FRAME_BODY_NED=8, /* Same as MAV_FRAME_LOCAL_NED when used to represent position values. Same as MAV_FRAME_BODY_FRD when used with velocity/acceleration values. | */
175 MAV_FRAME_BODY_OFFSET_NED=9, /* This is the same as MAV_FRAME_BODY_FRD. | */
176 MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL_TERRAIN_ALT=10, /* Global (WGS84) coordinate frame with AGL altitude (altitude at ground level). | */
177 MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL_TERRAIN_ALT_INT=11, /* Global (WGS84) coordinate frame (scaled) with AGL altitude (altitude at ground level). | */
178 MAV_FRAME_BODY_FRD=12, /* FRD local frame aligned to the vehicle's attitude (x: Forward, y: Right, z: Down) with an origin that travels with vehicle. | */
179 MAV_FRAME_RESERVED_13=13, /* MAV_FRAME_BODY_FLU - Body fixed frame of reference, Z-up (x: Forward, y: Left, z: Up). | */
180 MAV_FRAME_RESERVED_14=14, /* MAV_FRAME_MOCAP_NED - Odometry local coordinate frame of data given by a motion capture system, Z-down (x: North, y: East, z: Down). | */
181 MAV_FRAME_RESERVED_15=15, /* MAV_FRAME_MOCAP_ENU - Odometry local coordinate frame of data given by a motion capture system, Z-up (x: East, y: North, z: Up). | */
182 MAV_FRAME_RESERVED_16=16, /* MAV_FRAME_VISION_NED - Odometry local coordinate frame of data given by a vision estimation system, Z-down (x: North, y: East, z: Down). | */
183 MAV_FRAME_RESERVED_17=17, /* MAV_FRAME_VISION_ENU - Odometry local coordinate frame of data given by a vision estimation system, Z-up (x: East, y: North, z: Up). | */
184 MAV_FRAME_RESERVED_18=18, /* MAV_FRAME_ESTIM_NED - Odometry local coordinate frame of data given by an estimator running onboard the vehicle, Z-down (x: North, y: East, z: Down). | */
185 MAV_FRAME_RESERVED_19=19, /* MAV_FRAME_ESTIM_ENU - Odometry local coordinate frame of data given by an estimator running onboard the vehicle, Z-up (x: East, y: North, z: Up). | */
186 MAV_FRAME_LOCAL_FRD=20, /* FRD local tangent frame (x: Forward, y: Right, z: Down) with origin fixed relative to earth. The forward axis is aligned to the front of the vehicle in the horizontal plane. | */
187 MAV_FRAME_LOCAL_FLU=21, /* FLU local tangent frame (x: Forward, y: Left, z: Up) with origin fixed relative to earth. The forward axis is aligned to the front of the vehicle in the horizontal plane. | */
188 MAV_FRAME_ENUM_END=22, /* | */
189} MAV_FRAME;
190#endif
191
192/** @brief */
193#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE
194#define HAVE_ENUM_MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE
195typedef enum MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE
196{
197 MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_IMG_JPEG=0, /* | */
198 MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_IMG_BMP=1, /* | */
199 MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_IMG_RAW8U=2, /* | */
200 MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_IMG_RAW32U=3, /* | */
201 MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_IMG_PGM=4, /* | */
202 MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_IMG_PNG=5, /* | */
203 MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE_ENUM_END=6, /* | */
204} MAVLINK_DATA_STREAM_TYPE;
205#endif
206
207/** @brief */
208#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_BREACH
209#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_BREACH
210typedef enum FENCE_BREACH
211{
212 FENCE_BREACH_NONE=0, /* No last fence breach | */
213 FENCE_BREACH_MINALT=1, /* Breached minimum altitude | */
214 FENCE_BREACH_MAXALT=2, /* Breached maximum altitude | */
215 FENCE_BREACH_BOUNDARY=3, /* Breached fence boundary | */
216 FENCE_BREACH_ENUM_END=4, /* | */
217} FENCE_BREACH;
218#endif
219
220/** @brief Actions being taken to mitigate/prevent fence breach */
221#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_MITIGATE
222#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_MITIGATE
223typedef enum FENCE_MITIGATE
224{
225 FENCE_MITIGATE_UNKNOWN=0, /* Unknown | */
226 FENCE_MITIGATE_NONE=1, /* No actions being taken | */
227 FENCE_MITIGATE_VEL_LIMIT=2, /* Velocity limiting active to prevent breach | */
228 FENCE_MITIGATE_ENUM_END=3, /* | */
229} FENCE_MITIGATE;
230#endif
231
232/** @brief Fence types to enable or disable when using MAV_CMD_DO_FENCE_ENABLE.
233 Note that at least one of these flags must be set in MAV_CMD_DO_FENCE_ENABLE.param2.
234 If none are set, the flight stack will ignore the field and enable/disable its default set of fences (usually all of them).
235 */
236#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_TYPE
237#define HAVE_ENUM_FENCE_TYPE
238typedef enum FENCE_TYPE
239{
240 FENCE_TYPE_ALT_MAX=1, /* Maximum altitude fence | */
241 FENCE_TYPE_CIRCLE=2, /* Circle fence | */
242 FENCE_TYPE_POLYGON=4, /* Polygon fence | */
243 FENCE_TYPE_ALT_MIN=8, /* Minimum altitude fence | */
244 FENCE_TYPE_ENUM_END=9, /* | */
245} FENCE_TYPE;
246#endif
247
248/** @brief Enumeration of possible mount operation modes. This message is used by obsolete/deprecated gimbal messages. */
249#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MOUNT_MODE
250#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MOUNT_MODE
251typedef enum MAV_MOUNT_MODE
252{
253 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_RETRACT=0, /* Load and keep safe position (Roll,Pitch,Yaw) from permanent memory and stop stabilization | */
254 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_NEUTRAL=1, /* Load and keep neutral position (Roll,Pitch,Yaw) from permanent memory. | */
255 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_MAVLINK_TARGETING=2, /* Load neutral position and start MAVLink Roll,Pitch,Yaw control with stabilization | */
256 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_RC_TARGETING=3, /* Load neutral position and start RC Roll,Pitch,Yaw control with stabilization | */
257 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_GPS_POINT=4, /* Load neutral position and start to point to Lat,Lon,Alt | */
258 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_SYSID_TARGET=5, /* Gimbal tracks system with specified system ID | */
259 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_HOME_LOCATION=6, /* Gimbal tracks home position | */
260 MAV_MOUNT_MODE_ENUM_END=7, /* | */
261} MAV_MOUNT_MODE;
262#endif
263
264/** @brief Gimbal device (low level) capability flags (bitmap). */
265#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS
266#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS
267typedef enum GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS
268{
269 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_RETRACT=1, /* Gimbal device supports a retracted position. | */
270 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_NEUTRAL=2, /* Gimbal device supports a horizontal, forward looking position, stabilized. | */
271 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_AXIS=4, /* Gimbal device supports rotating around roll axis. | */
272 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_FOLLOW=8, /* Gimbal device supports to follow a roll angle relative to the vehicle. | */
273 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_LOCK=16, /* Gimbal device supports locking to a roll angle (generally that's the default with roll stabilized). | */
274 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_AXIS=32, /* Gimbal device supports rotating around pitch axis. | */
275 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_FOLLOW=64, /* Gimbal device supports to follow a pitch angle relative to the vehicle. | */
276 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_LOCK=128, /* Gimbal device supports locking to a pitch angle (generally that's the default with pitch stabilized). | */
277 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_AXIS=256, /* Gimbal device supports rotating around yaw axis. | */
278 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_FOLLOW=512, /* Gimbal device supports to follow a yaw angle relative to the vehicle (generally that's the default). | */
279 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_LOCK=1024, /* Gimbal device supports locking to an absolute heading, i.e., yaw angle relative to North (earth frame, often this is an option available). | */
280 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_SUPPORTS_INFINITE_YAW=2048, /* Gimbal device supports yawing/panning infinitely (e.g. using slip disk). | */
281 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_SUPPORTS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME=4096, /* Gimbal device supports yaw angles and angular velocities relative to North (earth frame). This usually requires support by an autopilot via AUTOPILOT_STATE_FOR_GIMBAL_DEVICE. Support can go on and off during runtime, which is reported by the flag GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_CAN_ACCEPT_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME. | */
282 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_RC_INPUTS=8192, /* Gimbal device supports radio control inputs as an alternative input for controlling the gimbal orientation. | */
283 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_CAN_POINT_LOCATION_LOCAL=65536, /* Gimbal device supports to point to a local position. | */
284 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_CAN_POINT_LOCATION_GLOBAL=131072, /* Gimbal device supports to point to a global latitude, longitude, altitude position. | */
285 GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_ENUM_END=131073, /* | */
286} GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS;
287#endif
288
289/** @brief Gimbal manager high level capability flags (bitmap). The flags are identical to the GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS. However, the gimbal manager does not need to copy the flags from the gimbal but can also enhance the capabilities and thus add flags. */
290#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS
291#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS
292typedef enum GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS
293{
294 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_RETRACT=1, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_RETRACT. | */
295 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_NEUTRAL=2, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_NEUTRAL. | */
296 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_AXIS=4, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_AXIS. | */
297 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_FOLLOW=8, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_FOLLOW. | */
298 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_LOCK=16, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_ROLL_LOCK. | */
299 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_AXIS=32, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_AXIS. | */
300 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_FOLLOW=64, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_FOLLOW. | */
301 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_LOCK=128, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_PITCH_LOCK. | */
302 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_AXIS=256, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_AXIS. | */
303 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_FOLLOW=512, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_FOLLOW. | */
304 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_LOCK=1024, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_YAW_LOCK. | */
305 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_SUPPORTS_INFINITE_YAW=2048, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_SUPPORTS_INFINITE_YAW. | */
306 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_SUPPORTS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME=4096, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_SUPPORTS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME. | */
307 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_RC_INPUTS=8192, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_RC_INPUTS. | */
308 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_CAN_POINT_LOCATION_LOCAL=65536, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_CAN_POINT_LOCATION_LOCAL. | */
309 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_CAN_POINT_LOCATION_GLOBAL=131072, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_CAP_FLAGS_CAN_POINT_LOCATION_GLOBAL. | */
310 GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS_ENUM_END=131073, /* | */
311} GIMBAL_MANAGER_CAP_FLAGS;
312#endif
313
314/** @brief Flags for gimbal device (lower level) operation. */
315#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS
316#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS
317typedef enum GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS
318{
319 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_RETRACT=1, /* Set to retracted safe position (no stabilization), takes precedence over all other flags. | */
320 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_NEUTRAL=2, /* Set to neutral/default position, taking precedence over all other flags except RETRACT. Neutral is commonly forward-facing and horizontal (roll=pitch=yaw=0) but may be any orientation. | */
321 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_ROLL_LOCK=4, /* Lock roll angle to absolute angle relative to horizon (not relative to vehicle). This is generally the default with a stabilizing gimbal. | */
322 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_PITCH_LOCK=8, /* Lock pitch angle to absolute angle relative to horizon (not relative to vehicle). This is generally the default with a stabilizing gimbal. | */
323 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_LOCK=16, /* Lock yaw angle to absolute angle relative to North (not relative to vehicle). If this flag is set, the yaw angle and z component of angular velocity are relative to North (earth frame, x-axis pointing North), else they are relative to the vehicle heading (vehicle frame, earth frame rotated so that the x-axis is pointing forward). | */
324 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_VEHICLE_FRAME=32, /* Yaw angle and z component of angular velocity are relative to the vehicle heading (vehicle frame, earth frame rotated such that the x-axis is pointing forward). | */
325 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME=64, /* Yaw angle and z component of angular velocity are relative to North (earth frame, x-axis is pointing North). | */
326 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_ACCEPTS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME=128, /* Gimbal device can accept yaw angle inputs relative to North (earth frame). This flag is only for reporting (attempts to set this flag are ignored). | */
327 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_RC_EXCLUSIVE=256, /* The gimbal orientation is set exclusively by the RC signals feed to the gimbal's radio control inputs. MAVLink messages for setting the gimbal orientation (GIMBAL_DEVICE_SET_ATTITUDE) are ignored. | */
328 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_RC_MIXED=512, /* The gimbal orientation is determined by combining/mixing the RC signals feed to the gimbal's radio control inputs and the MAVLink messages for setting the gimbal orientation (GIMBAL_DEVICE_SET_ATTITUDE). How these two controls are combined or mixed is not defined by the protocol but is up to the implementation. | */
329 GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_ENUM_END=513, /* | */
330} GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS;
331#endif
332
333/** @brief Flags for high level gimbal manager operation The first 16 bits are identical to the GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS. */
334#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS
335#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS
336typedef enum GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS
337{
338 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_RETRACT=1, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_RETRACT. | */
339 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_NEUTRAL=2, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_NEUTRAL. | */
340 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_ROLL_LOCK=4, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_ROLL_LOCK. | */
341 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_PITCH_LOCK=8, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_PITCH_LOCK. | */
342 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_YAW_LOCK=16, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_LOCK. | */
343 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_YAW_IN_VEHICLE_FRAME=32, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_VEHICLE_FRAME. | */
344 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME=64, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME. | */
345 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_ACCEPTS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME=128, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_ACCEPTS_YAW_IN_EARTH_FRAME. | */
346 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_RC_EXCLUSIVE=256, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_RC_EXCLUSIVE. | */
347 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_RC_MIXED=512, /* Based on GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_RC_MIXED. | */
348 GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS_ENUM_END=513, /* | */
349} GIMBAL_MANAGER_FLAGS;
350#endif
351
352/** @brief Gimbal device (low level) error flags (bitmap, 0 means no error) */
353#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS
354#define HAVE_ENUM_GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS
355typedef enum GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS
356{
357 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_AT_ROLL_LIMIT=1, /* Gimbal device is limited by hardware roll limit. | */
358 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_AT_PITCH_LIMIT=2, /* Gimbal device is limited by hardware pitch limit. | */
359 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_AT_YAW_LIMIT=4, /* Gimbal device is limited by hardware yaw limit. | */
360 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_ENCODER_ERROR=8, /* There is an error with the gimbal encoders. | */
361 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_POWER_ERROR=16, /* There is an error with the gimbal power source. | */
362 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_MOTOR_ERROR=32, /* There is an error with the gimbal motors. | */
363 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_SOFTWARE_ERROR=64, /* There is an error with the gimbal's software. | */
364 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_COMMS_ERROR=128, /* There is an error with the gimbal's communication. | */
365 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_CALIBRATION_RUNNING=256, /* Gimbal device is currently calibrating. | */
366 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_NO_MANAGER=512, /* Gimbal device is not assigned to a gimbal manager. | */
367 GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS_ENUM_END=513, /* | */
368} GIMBAL_DEVICE_ERROR_FLAGS;
369#endif
370
371/** @brief Gripper actions. */
372#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_GRIPPER_ACTIONS
373#define HAVE_ENUM_GRIPPER_ACTIONS
374typedef enum GRIPPER_ACTIONS
375{
376 GRIPPER_ACTION_RELEASE=0, /* Gripper release cargo. | */
377 GRIPPER_ACTION_GRAB=1, /* Gripper grab onto cargo. | */
378 GRIPPER_ACTION_HOLD=2, /* Gripper hold current grip state/position. | */
379 GRIPPER_ACTIONS_ENUM_END=3, /* | */
380} GRIPPER_ACTIONS;
381#endif
382
383/** @brief Winch actions. */
384#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_WINCH_ACTIONS
385#define HAVE_ENUM_WINCH_ACTIONS
386typedef enum WINCH_ACTIONS
387{
388 WINCH_RELAXED=0, /* Allow motor to freewheel. | */
389 WINCH_RELATIVE_LENGTH_CONTROL=1, /* Wind or unwind specified length of line, optionally using specified rate. | */
390 WINCH_RATE_CONTROL=2, /* Wind or unwind line at specified rate. | */
391 WINCH_LOCK=3, /* Perform the locking sequence to relieve motor while in the fully retracted position. Only action and instance command parameters are used, others are ignored. | */
392 WINCH_DELIVER=4, /* Sequence of drop, slow down, touch down, reel up, lock. Only action and instance command parameters are used, others are ignored. | */
393 WINCH_HOLD=5, /* Engage motor and hold current position. Only action and instance command parameters are used, others are ignored. | */
394 WINCH_RETRACT=6, /* Return the reel to the fully retracted position. Only action and instance command parameters are used, others are ignored. | */
395 WINCH_LOAD_LINE=7, /* Load the reel with line. The winch will calculate the total loaded length and stop when the tension exceeds a threshold. Only action and instance command parameters are used, others are ignored. | */
396 WINCH_ABANDON_LINE=8, /* Spool out the entire length of the line. Only action and instance command parameters are used, others are ignored. | */
397 WINCH_LOAD_PAYLOAD=9, /* Spools out just enough to present the hook to the user to load the payload. Only action and instance command parameters are used, others are ignored | */
398 WINCH_ACTIONS_ENUM_END=10, /* | */
399} WINCH_ACTIONS;
400#endif
401
402/** @brief Generalized UAVCAN node health */
403#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH
404#define HAVE_ENUM_UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH
405typedef enum UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH
406{
407 UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH_OK=0, /* The node is functioning properly. | */
408 UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH_WARNING=1, /* A critical parameter went out of range or the node has encountered a minor failure. | */
409 UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH_ERROR=2, /* The node has encountered a major failure. | */
410 UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH_CRITICAL=3, /* The node has suffered a fatal malfunction. | */
411 UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH_ENUM_END=4, /* | */
412} UAVCAN_NODE_HEALTH;
413#endif
414
415/** @brief Generalized UAVCAN node mode */
416#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_UAVCAN_NODE_MODE
417#define HAVE_ENUM_UAVCAN_NODE_MODE
418typedef enum UAVCAN_NODE_MODE
419{
420 UAVCAN_NODE_MODE_OPERATIONAL=0, /* The node is performing its primary functions. | */
421 UAVCAN_NODE_MODE_INITIALIZATION=1, /* The node is initializing; this mode is entered immediately after startup. | */
422 UAVCAN_NODE_MODE_MAINTENANCE=2, /* The node is under maintenance. | */
423 UAVCAN_NODE_MODE_SOFTWARE_UPDATE=3, /* The node is in the process of updating its software. | */
424 UAVCAN_NODE_MODE_OFFLINE=7, /* The node is no longer available online. | */
425 UAVCAN_NODE_MODE_ENUM_END=8, /* | */
426} UAVCAN_NODE_MODE;
427#endif
428
429/** @brief Indicates the ESC connection type. */
430#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE
431#define HAVE_ENUM_ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE
432typedef enum ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE
433{
434 ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE_PPM=0, /* Traditional PPM ESC. | */
435 ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE_SERIAL=1, /* Serial Bus connected ESC. | */
436 ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE_ONESHOT=2, /* One Shot PPM ESC. | */
437 ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE_I2C=3, /* I2C ESC. | */
438 ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE_CAN=4, /* CAN-Bus ESC. | */
439 ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE_DSHOT=5, /* DShot ESC. | */
440 ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE_ENUM_END=6, /* | */
441} ESC_CONNECTION_TYPE;
442#endif
443
444/** @brief Flags to report ESC failures. */
445#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS
446#define HAVE_ENUM_ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS
447typedef enum ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS
448{
449 ESC_FAILURE_OVER_CURRENT=1, /* Over current failure. | */
450 ESC_FAILURE_OVER_VOLTAGE=2, /* Over voltage failure. | */
451 ESC_FAILURE_OVER_TEMPERATURE=4, /* Over temperature failure. | */
452 ESC_FAILURE_OVER_RPM=8, /* Over RPM failure. | */
453 ESC_FAILURE_INCONSISTENT_CMD=16, /* Inconsistent command failure i.e. out of bounds. | */
454 ESC_FAILURE_MOTOR_STUCK=32, /* Motor stuck failure. | */
455 ESC_FAILURE_GENERIC=64, /* Generic ESC failure. | */
456 ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS_ENUM_END=65, /* | */
457} ESC_FAILURE_FLAGS;
458#endif
459
460/** @brief Flags to indicate the status of camera storage. */
461#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_STATUS
462#define HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_STATUS
463typedef enum STORAGE_STATUS
464{
465 STORAGE_STATUS_EMPTY=0, /* Storage is missing (no microSD card loaded for example.) | */
466 STORAGE_STATUS_UNFORMATTED=1, /* Storage present but unformatted. | */
467 STORAGE_STATUS_READY=2, /* Storage present and ready. | */
468 STORAGE_STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED=3, /* Camera does not supply storage status information. Capacity information in STORAGE_INFORMATION fields will be ignored. | */
469 STORAGE_STATUS_ENUM_END=4, /* | */
470} STORAGE_STATUS;
471#endif
472
473/** @brief Flags to indicate the type of storage. */
474#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_TYPE
475#define HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_TYPE
476typedef enum STORAGE_TYPE
477{
478 STORAGE_TYPE_UNKNOWN=0, /* Storage type is not known. | */
479 STORAGE_TYPE_USB_STICK=1, /* Storage type is USB device. | */
480 STORAGE_TYPE_SD=2, /* Storage type is SD card. | */
481 STORAGE_TYPE_MICROSD=3, /* Storage type is microSD card. | */
482 STORAGE_TYPE_CF=4, /* Storage type is CFast. | */
483 STORAGE_TYPE_CFE=5, /* Storage type is CFexpress. | */
484 STORAGE_TYPE_XQD=6, /* Storage type is XQD. | */
485 STORAGE_TYPE_HD=7, /* Storage type is HD mass storage type. | */
486 STORAGE_TYPE_OTHER=254, /* Storage type is other, not listed type. | */
487 STORAGE_TYPE_ENUM_END=255, /* | */
488} STORAGE_TYPE;
489#endif
490
491/** @brief Flags to indicate usage for a particular storage (see STORAGE_INFORMATION.storage_usage and MAV_CMD_SET_STORAGE_USAGE). */
492#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG
493#define HAVE_ENUM_STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG
494typedef enum STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG
495{
496 STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG_SET=1, /* Always set to 1 (indicates STORAGE_INFORMATION.storage_usage is supported). | */
497 STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG_PHOTO=2, /* Storage for saving photos. | */
498 STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG_VIDEO=4, /* Storage for saving videos. | */
499 STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG_LOGS=8, /* Storage for saving logs. | */
500 STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG_ENUM_END=9, /* | */
501} STORAGE_USAGE_FLAG;
502#endif
503
504/** @brief Yaw behaviour during orbit flight. */
505#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR
506#define HAVE_ENUM_ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR
507typedef enum ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR
508{
509 ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR_HOLD_FRONT_TO_CIRCLE_CENTER=0, /* Vehicle front points to the center (default). | */
510 ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR_HOLD_INITIAL_HEADING=1, /* Vehicle front holds heading when message received. | */
511 ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR_UNCONTROLLED=2, /* Yaw uncontrolled. | */
512 ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR_HOLD_FRONT_TANGENT_TO_CIRCLE=3, /* Vehicle front follows flight path (tangential to circle). | */
513 ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR_RC_CONTROLLED=4, /* Yaw controlled by RC input. | */
514 ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR_UNCHANGED=5, /* Vehicle uses current yaw behaviour (unchanged). The vehicle-default yaw behaviour is used if this value is specified when orbit is first commanded. | */
515 ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR_ENUM_END=6, /* | */
516} ORBIT_YAW_BEHAVIOUR;
517#endif
518
519/** @brief Possible responses from a WIFI_CONFIG_AP message. */
520#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE
521#define HAVE_ENUM_WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE
522typedef enum WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE
523{
524 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE_UNDEFINED=0, /* Undefined response. Likely an indicative of a system that doesn't support this request. | */
525 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE_ACCEPTED=1, /* Changes accepted. | */
526 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE_REJECTED=2, /* Changes rejected. | */
527 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE_MODE_ERROR=3, /* Invalid Mode. | */
528 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE_SSID_ERROR=4, /* Invalid SSID. | */
529 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE_PASSWORD_ERROR=5, /* Invalid Password. | */
530 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE_ENUM_END=6, /* | */
531} WIFI_CONFIG_AP_RESPONSE;
532#endif
533
534/** @brief Possible responses from a CELLULAR_CONFIG message. */
535#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE
536#define HAVE_ENUM_CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE
537typedef enum CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE
538{
539 CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE_ACCEPTED=0, /* Changes accepted. | */
540 CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE_APN_ERROR=1, /* Invalid APN. | */
541 CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE_PIN_ERROR=2, /* Invalid PIN. | */
542 CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE_REJECTED=3, /* Changes rejected. | */
543 CELLULAR_CONFIG_BLOCKED_PUK_REQUIRED=4, /* PUK is required to unblock SIM card. | */
544 CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
545} CELLULAR_CONFIG_RESPONSE;
546#endif
547
548/** @brief WiFi Mode. */
549#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE
550#define HAVE_ENUM_WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE
551typedef enum WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE
552{
553 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE_UNDEFINED=0, /* WiFi mode is undefined. | */
554 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE_AP=1, /* WiFi configured as an access point. | */
555 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE_STATION=2, /* WiFi configured as a station connected to an existing local WiFi network. | */
556 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE_DISABLED=3, /* WiFi disabled. | */
557 WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE_ENUM_END=4, /* | */
558} WIFI_CONFIG_AP_MODE;
559#endif
560
561/** @brief Supported component metadata types. These are used in the "general" metadata file returned by COMPONENT_METADATA to provide information about supported metadata types. The types are not used directly in MAVLink messages. */
562#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_COMP_METADATA_TYPE
563#define HAVE_ENUM_COMP_METADATA_TYPE
564typedef enum COMP_METADATA_TYPE
565{
566 COMP_METADATA_TYPE_GENERAL=0, /* General information about the component. General metadata includes information about other metadata types supported by the component. Files of this type must be supported, and must be downloadable from vehicle using a MAVLink FTP URI. | */
567 COMP_METADATA_TYPE_PARAMETER=1, /* Parameter meta data. | */
568 COMP_METADATA_TYPE_COMMANDS=2, /* Meta data that specifies which commands and command parameters the vehicle supports. (WIP) | */
569 COMP_METADATA_TYPE_PERIPHERALS=3, /* Meta data that specifies external non-MAVLink peripherals. | */
570 COMP_METADATA_TYPE_EVENTS=4, /* Meta data for the events interface. | */
571 COMP_METADATA_TYPE_ACTUATORS=5, /* Meta data for actuator configuration (motors, servos and vehicle geometry) and testing. | */
572 COMP_METADATA_TYPE_ENUM_END=6, /* | */
573} COMP_METADATA_TYPE;
574#endif
575
576/** @brief Actuator configuration, used to change a setting on an actuator. Component information metadata can be used to know which outputs support which commands. */
577#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION
578#define HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION
579typedef enum ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION
580{
581 ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION_NONE=0, /* Do nothing. | */
582 ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION_BEEP=1, /* Command the actuator to beep now. | */
583 ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION_3D_MODE_ON=2, /* Permanently set the actuator (ESC) to 3D mode (reversible thrust). | */
584 ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION_3D_MODE_OFF=3, /* Permanently set the actuator (ESC) to non 3D mode (non-reversible thrust). | */
585 ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION_SPIN_DIRECTION1=4, /* Permanently set the actuator (ESC) to spin direction 1 (which can be clockwise or counter-clockwise). | */
586 ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION_SPIN_DIRECTION2=5, /* Permanently set the actuator (ESC) to spin direction 2 (opposite of direction 1). | */
587 ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION_ENUM_END=6, /* | */
588} ACTUATOR_CONFIGURATION;
589#endif
590
591/** @brief Actuator output function. Values greater or equal to 1000 are autopilot-specific. */
592#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION
593#define HAVE_ENUM_ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION
594typedef enum ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION
595{
596 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_NONE=0, /* No function (disabled). | */
597 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR1=1, /* Motor 1 | */
598 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR2=2, /* Motor 2 | */
599 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR3=3, /* Motor 3 | */
600 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR4=4, /* Motor 4 | */
601 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR5=5, /* Motor 5 | */
602 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR6=6, /* Motor 6 | */
603 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR7=7, /* Motor 7 | */
604 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR8=8, /* Motor 8 | */
605 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR9=9, /* Motor 9 | */
606 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR10=10, /* Motor 10 | */
607 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR11=11, /* Motor 11 | */
608 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR12=12, /* Motor 12 | */
609 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR13=13, /* Motor 13 | */
610 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR14=14, /* Motor 14 | */
611 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR15=15, /* Motor 15 | */
612 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_MOTOR16=16, /* Motor 16 | */
613 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO1=33, /* Servo 1 | */
614 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO2=34, /* Servo 2 | */
615 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO3=35, /* Servo 3 | */
616 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO4=36, /* Servo 4 | */
617 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO5=37, /* Servo 5 | */
618 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO6=38, /* Servo 6 | */
619 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO7=39, /* Servo 7 | */
620 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO8=40, /* Servo 8 | */
621 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO9=41, /* Servo 9 | */
622 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO10=42, /* Servo 10 | */
623 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO11=43, /* Servo 11 | */
624 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO12=44, /* Servo 12 | */
625 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO13=45, /* Servo 13 | */
626 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO14=46, /* Servo 14 | */
627 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO15=47, /* Servo 15 | */
628 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_SERVO16=48, /* Servo 16 | */
629 ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION_ENUM_END=49, /* | */
630} ACTUATOR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION;
631#endif
632
633/** @brief Axes that will be autotuned by MAV_CMD_DO_AUTOTUNE_ENABLE.
634 Note that at least one flag must be set in MAV_CMD_DO_AUTOTUNE_ENABLE.param2: if none are set, the flight stack will tune its default set of axes. */
635#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_AUTOTUNE_AXIS
636#define HAVE_ENUM_AUTOTUNE_AXIS
637typedef enum AUTOTUNE_AXIS
638{
639 AUTOTUNE_AXIS_ROLL=1, /* Autotune roll axis. | */
640 AUTOTUNE_AXIS_PITCH=2, /* Autotune pitch axis. | */
641 AUTOTUNE_AXIS_YAW=4, /* Autotune yaw axis. | */
642 AUTOTUNE_AXIS_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
643} AUTOTUNE_AXIS;
644#endif
645
646/** @brief
647 Actions for reading/writing parameters between persistent and volatile storage when using MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE.
648 (Commonly parameters are loaded from persistent storage (flash/EEPROM) into volatile storage (RAM) on startup and written back when they are changed.)
649 */
650#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION
651#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION
652typedef enum PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION
653{
654 PARAM_READ_PERSISTENT=0, /* Read all parameters from persistent storage. Replaces values in volatile storage. | */
655 PARAM_WRITE_PERSISTENT=1, /* Write all parameter values to persistent storage (flash/EEPROM) | */
656 PARAM_RESET_FACTORY_DEFAULT=2, /* Reset parameters to default values (such as sensor calibration, safety settings, and so on). Note that a flight stack may choose not to reset some parameters at their own discretion (such as those that are locked or expected to persist for the vehicle lifetime). | */
657 PARAM_RESET_SENSOR_DEFAULT=3, /* Reset only sensor calibration parameters to factory defaults (or firmware default if not available) | */
658 PARAM_RESET_ALL_DEFAULT=4, /* Reset all parameters to default values. | */
659 PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
660} PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_PARAMETER_ACTION;
661#endif
662
663/** @brief
664 Actions for reading and writing plan information (mission, rally points, geofence) between persistent and volatile storage when using MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE.
665 (Commonly missions are loaded from persistent storage (flash/EEPROM) into volatile storage (RAM) on startup and written back when they are changed.)
666 */
667#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION
668#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION
669typedef enum PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION
670{
671 MISSION_READ_PERSISTENT=0, /* Read current mission data from persistent storage | */
672 MISSION_WRITE_PERSISTENT=1, /* Write current mission data to persistent storage | */
673 MISSION_RESET_DEFAULT=2, /* Erase all mission data stored on the vehicle (both persistent and volatile storage) | */
674 PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION_ENUM_END=3, /* | */
675} PREFLIGHT_STORAGE_MISSION_ACTION;
676#endif
677
678/** @brief Reboot/shutdown action for selected component in MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN. */
679#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION
680#define HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION
681typedef enum REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION
682{
683 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION_NONE=0, /* Do nothing. | */
684 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION_REBOOT=1, /* Reboot component. | */
685 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION_SHUTDOWN=2, /* Shutdown component. | */
686 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION_REBOOT_TO_BOOTLOADER=3, /* Reboot component and keep it in the bootloader until upgraded. | */
687 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION_POWER_ON=4, /* Power on component. Do nothing if component is already powered (ACK command with MAV_RESULT_ACCEPTED). | */
688 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
689} REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_ACTION;
690#endif
691
692/** @brief Specifies the conditions under which the MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN command should be accepted. */
693#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS
694#define HAVE_ENUM_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS
695typedef enum REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS
696{
697 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS_SAFETY_INTERLOCKED=0, /* Reboot/Shutdown only if allowed by safety checks, such as being landed. | */
698 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS_FORCE=20190226, /* Force reboot/shutdown of the autopilot/component regardless of system state. | */
699 REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS_ENUM_END=20190227, /* | */
700} REBOOT_SHUTDOWN_CONDITIONS;
701#endif
702
703/** @brief Action for the magnetometer (param2) of MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION. */
704#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER
705#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER
706typedef enum PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER
707{
708 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER_NONE=0, /* No action. | */
709 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER_START=1, /* Start magnetometer calibration. | */
710 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER_FORCE_SAVE=76, /* Force-accept the existing compass calibration as valid without re-running it. Useful after a parameter reload that cleared calibration validity flags. | */
711 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER_ENUM_END=77, /* | */
712} PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_MAGNETOMETER;
713#endif
714
715/** @brief Action for the accelerometer (param5) of MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION. */
716#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER
717#define HAVE_ENUM_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER
718typedef enum PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER
719{
720 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER_NONE=0, /* No action. | */
721 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER_FULL=1, /* Full 6-position accelerometer calibration. | */
722 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER_TRIM=2, /* Board level (trim) calibration. | */
723 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER_TEMPERATURE=3, /* Accelerometer temperature calibration. | */
724 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER_SIMPLE=4, /* Simple accelerometer calibration. | */
725 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER_FORCE_SAVE=76, /* Force-accept the existing accelerometer calibration as valid without re-running it. Useful after a parameter reload that cleared calibration validity flags. | */
726 PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER_ENUM_END=77, /* | */
727} PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION_ACCELEROMETER;
728#endif
729
730/** @brief */
731#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS
732#define HAVE_ENUM_NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS
733typedef enum NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS
734{
735 NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS_HORIZONTAL_POSITION_NOT_REQUIRED=1, /* Accept the command even if the autopilot does not have control over its horizontal position (note that it might not have altitude control either). | */
736 NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS_ENUM_END=2, /* | */
737} NAV_TAKEOFF_FLAGS;
738#endif
739
740/** @brief Commands to be executed by the MAV. They can be executed on user request, or as part of a mission script. If the action is used in a mission, the parameter mapping to the waypoint/mission message is as follows: Param 1, Param 2, Param 3, Param 4, X: Param 5, Y:Param 6, Z:Param 7. This command list is similar what ARINC 424 is for commercial aircraft: A data format how to interpret waypoint/mission data. NaN and INT32_MAX may be used in float/integer params (respectively) to indicate optional/default values (e.g. to use the component's current yaw or latitude rather than a specific value). See https://mavlink.io/en/guide/xml_schema.html#MAV_CMD for information about the structure of the MAV_CMD entries */
741#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_CMD
742#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_CMD
743typedef enum MAV_CMD
744{
745 MAV_CMD_NAV_WAYPOINT=16, /* Navigate to waypoint. This is intended for use in missions (for guided commands outside of missions use MAV_CMD_DO_REPOSITION). |Hold time. (ignored by fixed wing, time to stay at waypoint for rotary wing)| Acceptance radius (if the sphere with this radius is hit, the waypoint counts as reached)| 0 to pass through the WP, if > 0 radius to pass by WP. Positive value for clockwise orbit, negative value for counter-clockwise orbit. Allows trajectory control.| Desired yaw angle at waypoint (rotary wing). NaN to use the current system yaw heading mode (e.g. yaw towards next waypoint, yaw to home, etc.).| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
746 MAV_CMD_NAV_LOITER_UNLIM=17, /* Loiter around this waypoint an unlimited amount of time |Empty| Empty| Loiter radius around waypoint for forward-only moving vehicles (not multicopters). If positive loiter clockwise, else counter-clockwise| Desired yaw angle. NaN to use the current system yaw heading mode (e.g. yaw towards next waypoint, yaw to home, etc.).| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
747 MAV_CMD_NAV_LOITER_TURNS=18, /* Loiter around this waypoint for X turns |Number of turns.| Leave loiter circle only when track heads towards the next waypoint (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: Leave when turns complete). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Loiter radius around waypoint for forward-only moving vehicles (not multicopters). If positive loiter clockwise, else counter-clockwise| Loiter circle exit location and/or path to next waypoint ("xtrack") for forward-only moving vehicles (not multicopters). 0 for the vehicle to converge towards the center xtrack when it leaves the loiter (the line between the centers of the current and next waypoint), 1 to converge to the direct line between the location that the vehicle exits the loiter radius and the next waypoint. Otherwise the angle (in degrees) between the tangent of the loiter circle and the center xtrack at which the vehicle must leave the loiter (and converge to the center xtrack). NaN to use the current system default xtrack behaviour.| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
748 MAV_CMD_NAV_LOITER_TIME=19, /* Loiter at the specified latitude, longitude and altitude for a certain amount of time. Multicopter vehicles stop at the point (within a vehicle-specific acceptance radius). Forward-only moving vehicles (e.g. fixed-wing) circle the point with the specified radius/direction. If the Heading Required parameter (2) is non-zero forward moving aircraft will only leave the loiter circle once heading towards the next waypoint. |Loiter time (only starts once Lat, Lon and Alt is reached).| Leave loiter circle only when track heading towards the next waypoint (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: Leave on time expiry). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Loiter radius around waypoint for forward-only moving vehicles (not multicopters). If positive loiter clockwise, else counter-clockwise.| Loiter circle exit location and/or path to next waypoint ("xtrack") for forward-only moving vehicles (not multicopters). 0 for the vehicle to converge towards the center xtrack when it leaves the loiter (the line between the centers of the current and next waypoint), 1 to converge to the direct line between the location that the vehicle exits the loiter radius and the next waypoint. Otherwise the angle (in degrees) between the tangent of the loiter circle and the center xtrack at which the vehicle must leave the loiter (and converge to the center xtrack). NaN to use the current system default xtrack behaviour.| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
749 MAV_CMD_NAV_RETURN_TO_LAUNCH=20, /* Return to launch location |Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
750 MAV_CMD_NAV_LAND=21, /* Land at location. |Minimum target altitude if landing is aborted (0 = undefined/use system default).| Precision land mode.| Empty.| Desired yaw angle. NaN to use the current system yaw heading mode (e.g. yaw towards next waypoint, yaw to home, etc.).| Latitude.| Longitude.| Landing altitude (ground level in current frame).| */
751 MAV_CMD_NAV_TAKEOFF=22, /* Takeoff from ground / hand. Vehicles that support multiple takeoff modes (e.g. VTOL quadplane) should take off using the currently configured mode. |Minimum pitch (if airspeed sensor present), desired pitch without sensor| Empty| Bitmask of options flags.| Yaw angle (if magnetometer present), ignored without magnetometer. NaN to use the current system yaw heading mode (e.g. yaw towards next waypoint, yaw to home, etc.).| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
752 MAV_CMD_NAV_LAND_LOCAL=23, /* Land at local position (local frame only) |Landing target number (if available)| Maximum accepted offset from desired landing position - computed magnitude from spherical coordinates: d = sqrt(x^2 + y^2 + z^2), which gives the maximum accepted distance between the desired landing position and the position where the vehicle is about to land| Landing descend rate| Desired yaw angle| Y-axis position| X-axis position| Z-axis / ground level position| */
753 MAV_CMD_NAV_TAKEOFF_LOCAL=24, /* Takeoff from local position (local frame only) |Minimum pitch (if airspeed sensor present), desired pitch without sensor| Empty| Takeoff ascend rate| Yaw angle (if magnetometer or another yaw estimation source present), ignored without one of these| Y-axis position| X-axis position| Z-axis position| */
754 MAV_CMD_NAV_FOLLOW=25, /* Vehicle following, i.e. this waypoint represents the position of a moving vehicle |Following logic to use (e.g. loitering or sinusoidal following) - depends on specific autopilot implementation| Ground speed of vehicle to be followed| Radius around waypoint. If positive loiter clockwise, else counter-clockwise| Desired yaw angle.| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
755 MAV_CMD_NAV_CONTINUE_AND_CHANGE_ALT=30, /* Continue on the current course and climb/descend to specified altitude. When the altitude is reached continue to the next command (i.e., don't proceed to the next command until the desired altitude is reached. |Climb or Descend (0 = Neutral, command completes when within 5m of this command's altitude, 1 = Climbing, command completes when at or above this command's altitude, 2 = Descending, command completes when at or below this command's altitude.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Desired altitude| */
756 MAV_CMD_NAV_LOITER_TO_ALT=31, /* Begin loiter at the specified Latitude and Longitude. If Lat=Lon=0, then loiter at the current position. Don't consider the navigation command complete (don't leave loiter) until the altitude has been reached. Additionally, if the Heading Required parameter is non-zero the aircraft will not leave the loiter until heading toward the next waypoint. |Leave loiter circle only when track heading towards the next waypoint (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: Leave when altitude reached). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Loiter radius around waypoint for forward-only moving vehicles (not multicopters). If positive loiter clockwise, negative counter-clockwise, 0 means no change to standard loiter.| Empty| Loiter circle exit location and/or path to next waypoint ("xtrack") for forward-only moving vehicles (not multicopters). 0 for the vehicle to converge towards the center xtrack when it leaves the loiter (the line between the centers of the current and next waypoint), 1 to converge to the direct line between the location that the vehicle exits the loiter radius and the next waypoint. Otherwise the angle (in degrees) between the tangent of the loiter circle and the center xtrack at which the vehicle must leave the loiter (and converge to the center xtrack). NaN to use the current system default xtrack behaviour.| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
757 MAV_CMD_DO_FOLLOW=32, /* Begin following a target |System ID (of the FOLLOW_TARGET beacon). Send 0 to disable follow-me and return to the default position hold mode.| Reserved| Reserved| Altitude mode: 0: Keep current altitude, 1: keep altitude difference to target, 2: go to a fixed altitude above home.| Altitude above home. (used if mode=2)| Reserved| Time to land in which the MAV should go to the default position hold mode after a message RX timeout.| */
758 MAV_CMD_DO_FOLLOW_REPOSITION=33, /* Reposition the MAV after a follow target command has been sent |Camera q1 (where 0 is on the ray from the camera to the tracking device)| Camera q2| Camera q3| Camera q4| altitude offset from target| X offset from target| Y offset from target| */
759 MAV_CMD_DO_ORBIT=34, /* Start orbiting on the circumference of a circle defined by the parameters. Setting values to NaN/INT32_MAX (as appropriate) results in using defaults. |Radius of the circle. Positive: orbit clockwise. Negative: orbit counter-clockwise. NaN: Use vehicle default radius, or current radius if already orbiting.| Tangential Velocity. NaN: Use vehicle default velocity, or current velocity if already orbiting.| Yaw behavior of the vehicle.| Orbit around the centre point for this many radians (i.e. for a three-quarter orbit set 270*Pi/180). 0: Orbit forever. NaN: Use vehicle default, or current value if already orbiting.| Center point latitude (if no MAV_FRAME specified) / X coordinate according to MAV_FRAME. INT32_MAX (or NaN if sent in COMMAND_LONG): Use current vehicle position, or current center if already orbiting.| Center point longitude (if no MAV_FRAME specified) / Y coordinate according to MAV_FRAME. INT32_MAX (or NaN if sent in COMMAND_LONG): Use current vehicle position, or current center if already orbiting.| Center point altitude (MSL) (if no MAV_FRAME specified) / Z coordinate according to MAV_FRAME. NaN: Use current vehicle altitude.| */
760 MAV_CMD_DO_FIGURE_EIGHT=35, /* Fly a figure eight path as defined by the parameters.
761 Set parameters to NaN/INT32_MAX (as appropriate) to use system-default values.
762 The command is intended for fixed wing vehicles (and VTOL hybrids flying in fixed-wing mode), allowing POI tracking for gimbals that don't support infinite rotation.
763 This command only defines the flight path. Speed should be set independently (use e.g. MAV_CMD_DO_CHANGE_SPEED).
764 Yaw and other degrees of freedom are not specified, and will be flight-stack specific (on vehicles where they can be controlled independent of the heading).
765 |Major axis radius of the figure eight. Positive: orbit the north circle clockwise. Negative: orbit the north circle counter-clockwise.
766 NaN: The radius will be set to 2.5 times the minor radius and direction is clockwise.
767 Must be greater or equal to two times the minor radius for feasible values.| Minor axis radius of the figure eight. Defines the radius of the two circles that make up the figure. Negative value has no effect.
768 NaN: The radius will be set to the default loiter radius.| Reserved (default:NaN)| Orientation of the figure eight major axis with respect to true north (range: [-pi,pi]). NaN: use default orientation aligned to true north.| Center point latitude/X coordinate according to MAV_FRAME. If no MAV_FRAME specified, MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL is assumed.
769 INT32_MAX or NaN: Use current vehicle position, or current center if already loitering.| Center point longitude/Y coordinate according to MAV_FRAME. If no MAV_FRAME specified, MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL is assumed.
770 INT32_MAX or NaN: Use current vehicle position, or current center if already loitering.| Center point altitude MSL/Z coordinate according to MAV_FRAME. If no MAV_FRAME specified, MAV_FRAME_GLOBAL is assumed.
771 INT32_MAX or NaN: Use current vehicle altitude.| */
772 MAV_CMD_NAV_ARC_WAYPOINT=36, /* Circular arc path waypoint.
773 This defines the end/exit point and angle (param1) of an arc path from the previous waypoint. A position is required before this command to define the start of the arc (e.g. current position, a MAV_CMD_NAV_WAYPOINT, or a MAV_CMD_NAV_ARC_WAYPOINT).
774 The resulting path is a circular arc in the NE frame, with the difference in height being defined by the difference in waypoint altitudes.
775 |The angle in degrees from the starting position to the exit position of the arc in the NE frame. Positive values are CW arcs and negative values are CCW arcs.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
776 MAV_CMD_NAV_ROI=80, /* Sets the region of interest (ROI) for a sensor set or the vehicle itself. This can then be used by the vehicle's control system to control the vehicle attitude and the attitude of various sensors such as cameras. |Region of interest mode.| Waypoint index/ target ID. (see MAV_ROI enum)| ROI index (allows a vehicle to manage multiple ROI's)| Empty| x the location of the fixed ROI (see MAV_FRAME)| y| z| */
777 MAV_CMD_NAV_PATHPLANNING=81, /* Control autonomous path planning on the MAV. |0: Disable local obstacle avoidance / local path planning (without resetting map), 1: Enable local path planning, 2: Enable and reset local path planning| 0: Disable full path planning (without resetting map), 1: Enable, 2: Enable and reset map/occupancy grid, 3: Enable and reset planned route, but not occupancy grid| Empty| Yaw angle at goal| Latitude/X of goal| Longitude/Y of goal| Altitude/Z of goal| */
778 MAV_CMD_NAV_SPLINE_WAYPOINT=82, /* Navigate to waypoint using a spline path. |Hold time. (ignored by fixed wing, time to stay at waypoint for rotary wing)| Empty| Empty| Empty| Latitude/X of goal| Longitude/Y of goal| Altitude/Z of goal| */
779 MAV_CMD_NAV_VTOL_TAKEOFF=84, /* Takeoff from ground using VTOL mode, and transition to forward flight with specified heading. The command should be ignored by vehicles that dont support both VTOL and fixed-wing flight (multicopters, boats,etc.). |Empty| Front transition heading.| Empty| Yaw angle. NaN to use the current system yaw heading mode (e.g. yaw towards next waypoint, yaw to home, etc.).| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
780 MAV_CMD_NAV_VTOL_LAND=85, /* Land using VTOL mode |Landing behaviour.| Empty| Approach altitude (with the same reference as the Altitude field). NaN if unspecified.| Yaw angle. NaN to use the current system yaw heading mode (e.g. yaw towards next waypoint, yaw to home, etc.).| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude (ground level) relative to the current coordinate frame. NaN to use system default landing altitude (ignore value).| */
781 MAV_CMD_NAV_GUIDED_ENABLE=92, /* Hand control over to an external controller |Guided mode on (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: Off). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
782 MAV_CMD_NAV_DELAY=93, /* Delay the next navigation command a number of seconds or until a specified time |Delay (-1 to enable time-of-day fields)| hour (24h format, UTC, -1 to ignore)| minute (24h format, UTC, -1 to ignore)| second (24h format, UTC, -1 to ignore)| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
783 MAV_CMD_NAV_PAYLOAD_PLACE=94, /* Descend and place payload. Vehicle moves to specified location, descends until it detects a hanging payload has reached the ground, and then releases the payload. If ground is not detected before the reaching the maximum descent value (param1), the command will complete without releasing the payload. |Maximum distance to descend.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
784 MAV_CMD_NAV_LAST=95, /* NOP - This command is only used to mark the upper limit of the NAV/ACTION commands in the enumeration |Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
785 MAV_CMD_CONDITION_DELAY=112, /* Delay mission state machine. |Delay| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
786 MAV_CMD_CONDITION_CHANGE_ALT=113, /* Ascend/descend to target altitude at specified rate. Delay mission state machine until desired altitude reached. |Descent / Ascend rate.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Target Altitude| */
787 MAV_CMD_CONDITION_DISTANCE=114, /* Delay mission state machine until within desired distance of next NAV point. |Distance.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
788 MAV_CMD_CONDITION_YAW=115, /* Reach a certain target angle. |target angle [0-360]. Absolute angles: 0 is north. Relative angle: 0 is initial yaw. Direction set by param3.| angular speed| direction: -1: counter clockwise, 0: shortest direction, 1: clockwise| Relative offset (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: absolute angle). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
789 MAV_CMD_CONDITION_LAST=159, /* NOP - This command is only used to mark the upper limit of the CONDITION commands in the enumeration |Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
790 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_MODE=176, /* Set system mode. |Mode flags. MAV_MODE values can be used to set some mode flag combinations.| Custom system-specific mode (see target autopilot specifications for mode information). If MAV_MODE_FLAG_CUSTOM_MODE_ENABLED is set in param1 (mode) this mode is used: otherwise the field is ignored.| Custom sub mode - this is system specific, please refer to the individual autopilot specifications for details.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
791 MAV_CMD_DO_JUMP=177, /* Jump to the desired command in the mission list. Repeat this action only the specified number of times |Sequence number| Repeat count| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
792 MAV_CMD_DO_CHANGE_SPEED=178, /* Change speed and/or throttle set points. The value persists until it is overridden or there is a mode change |Speed type of value set in param2 (such as airspeed, ground speed, and so on)| Speed (-1 indicates no change, -2 indicates return to default vehicle speed)| Throttle (-1 indicates no change, -2 indicates return to default vehicle throttle value)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
793 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_HOME=179, /*
794 Sets the home position to either to the current position or a specified position.
795 The home position is the default position that the system will return to and land on.
796 The position is set automatically by the system during the takeoff (and may also be set using this command).
797 Note: the current home position may be emitted in a HOME_POSITION message on request (using MAV_CMD_REQUEST_MESSAGE with param1=242).
798 |Use current location (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: use specified location). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Roll angle (of surface). Range: -180..180 degrees. NAN or 0 means value not set. 0.01 indicates zero roll.| Pitch angle (of surface). Range: -90..90 degrees. NAN or 0 means value not set. 0.01 means zero pitch.| Yaw angle. NaN to use default heading. Range: -180..180 degrees.| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
799 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_PARAMETER=180, /* Set a system parameter. Caution! Use of this command requires knowledge of the numeric enumeration value of the parameter. |Parameter number| Parameter value| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
800 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_RELAY=181, /* Set a relay to a condition. The current value may optionally be reported using RELAY_STATUS. |Relay instance number.| Setting. (1=on, 0=off, others possible depending on system hardware)| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
801 MAV_CMD_DO_REPEAT_RELAY=182, /* Cycle a relay on and off for a desired number of cycles with a desired period. |Relay instance number.| Cycle count.| Cycle time.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
802 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_SERVO=183, /* Set a servo to a desired PWM value. |Servo instance number.| Pulse Width Modulation.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
803 MAV_CMD_DO_REPEAT_SERVO=184, /* Cycle a between its nominal setting and a desired PWM for a desired number of cycles with a desired period. |Servo instance number.| Pulse Width Modulation.| Cycle count.| Cycle time.| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
804 MAV_CMD_DO_FLIGHTTERMINATION=185, /* Terminate flight immediately.
805 Flight termination immediately and irreversibly terminates the current flight, returning the vehicle to ground.
806 The vehicle will ignore RC or other input until it has been power-cycled.
807 Termination may trigger safety measures, including: disabling motors and deployment of parachute on multicopters, and setting flight surfaces to initiate a landing pattern on fixed-wing).
808 On multicopters without a parachute it may trigger a crash landing.
809 Support for this command can be tested using the protocol bit: MAV_PROTOCOL_CAPABILITY_FLIGHT_TERMINATION.
810 Support for this command can also be tested by sending the command with param1=0 (< 0.5); the ACK should be either MAV_RESULT_FAILED or MAV_RESULT_UNSUPPORTED.
811 |Flight termination activated if > 0.5. Otherwise not activated and ACK with MAV_RESULT_FAILED.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
812 MAV_CMD_DO_CHANGE_ALTITUDE=186, /* Change altitude set point. |Altitude.| Frame of new altitude.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
813 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_ACTUATOR=187, /* Sets actuators (e.g. servos) to a desired value. The actuator numbers are mapped to specific outputs (e.g. on any MAIN or AUX PWM or UAVCAN) using a flight-stack specific mechanism (i.e. a parameter). |Actuator 1 value, scaled from [-1 to 1]. NaN to ignore.| Actuator 2 value, scaled from [-1 to 1]. NaN to ignore.| Actuator 3 value, scaled from [-1 to 1]. NaN to ignore.| Actuator 4 value, scaled from [-1 to 1]. NaN to ignore.| Actuator 5 value, scaled from [-1 to 1]. NaN to ignore.| Actuator 6 value, scaled from [-1 to 1]. NaN to ignore.| Index of actuator set (i.e if set to 1, Actuator 1 becomes Actuator 7)| */
814 MAV_CMD_DO_RETURN_PATH_START=188, /* Mission item to specify the start of a failsafe/landing return-path segment (the end of the segment is the next MAV_CMD_DO_LAND_START item).
815 A vehicle that is using missions for landing (e.g. in a return mode) will join the mission on the closest path of the return-path segment (instead of MAV_CMD_DO_LAND_START or the nearest waypoint).
816 The main use case is to minimize the failsafe flight path in corridor missions, where the inbound/outbound paths are constrained (by geofences) to the same particular path.
817 The MAV_CMD_NAV_RETURN_PATH_START would be placed at the start of the return path.
818 If a failsafe occurs on the outbound path the vehicle will move to the nearest point on the return path (which is parallel for this kind of mission), effectively turning round and following the shortest path to landing.
819 If a failsafe occurs on the inbound path the vehicle is already on the return segment and will continue to landing.
820 The Latitude/Longitude/Altitude are optional, and may be set to 0 if not needed.
821 If specified, the item defines the waypoint at which the return segment starts.
822 If sent using as a command, the vehicle will perform a mission landing (using the land segment if defined) or reject the command if mission landings are not supported, or no mission landing is defined. When used as a command any position information in the command is ignored.
823 |Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Latitudee. 0: not used.| Longitudee. 0: not used.| Altitudee. 0: not used.| */
824 MAV_CMD_DO_LAND_START=189, /* Mission item to mark the start of a mission landing pattern, or a command to land with a mission landing pattern.
825
826 When used in a mission, this is a marker for the start of a sequence of mission items that represent a landing pattern.
827 It should be followed by a navigation item that defines the first waypoint of the landing sequence.
828 The start marker positional params are used only for selecting what landing pattern to use if several are defined in the mission (the selected pattern will be the one with the marker position that is closest to the vehicle when a landing is commanded).
829 If the marker item position has zero-values for latitude, longitude, and altitude, then landing pattern selection is instead based on the position of the first waypoint in the landing sequence.
830
831 When sent as a command it triggers a landing using a mission landing pattern.
832 The location parameters are not used in this case, and should be set to 0.
833 |Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Latitude for landing sequence selection, or 0 (see description). Ignored in commands (set 0).| Longitude for landing sequence selection, or 0 (see description). Ignored in commands (set 0).| Altitude for landing sequence selection, or 0 (see description). Ignored in commands (set 0).| */
834 MAV_CMD_DO_RALLY_LAND=190, /* Mission command to perform a landing from a rally point. |Break altitude| Landing speed| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
835 MAV_CMD_DO_GO_AROUND=191, /* Mission command to safely abort an autonomous landing. |Altitude| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
836 MAV_CMD_DO_REPOSITION=192, /* Reposition the vehicle to a specific WGS84 global position. This command is intended for guided commands (for missions use MAV_CMD_NAV_WAYPOINT instead). |Ground speed, less than 0 (-1) for default| Bitmask of option flags.| Loiter radius for planes. Positive values only, direction is controlled by Yaw value. A value of zero or NaN is ignored. | Yaw heading (heading reference defined in Bitmask field). NaN to use the current system yaw heading mode (e.g. yaw towards next waypoint, yaw to home, etc.). For planes indicates loiter direction (0: clockwise, 1: counter clockwise)| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
837 MAV_CMD_DO_PAUSE_CONTINUE=193, /* If in a GPS controlled position mode, hold the current position or continue. |Continue mission (MAV_BOOL_TRUE), Pause current mission or reposition command, hold current position (MAV_BOOL_FALSE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid. A VTOL capable vehicle should enter hover mode (multicopter and VTOL planes). A plane should loiter with the default loiter radius.| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| */
838 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_REVERSE=194, /* Set moving direction to forward or reverse. |Reverse direction (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: Forward direction). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
839 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_ROI_LOCATION=195, /* Sets the region of interest (ROI) to a location. This can then be used by the vehicle's control system to control the vehicle attitude and the attitude of various sensors such as cameras. This command can be sent to a gimbal manager but not to a gimbal device. A gimbal is not to react to this message. |Component ID of gimbal device to address (or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal), 0 for all gimbal device components. Send command multiple times for more than one gimbal (but not all gimbals).| Empty| Empty| Empty| Latitude of ROI location| Longitude of ROI location| Altitude of ROI location| */
840 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_ROI_WPNEXT_OFFSET=196, /* Sets the region of interest (ROI) to be toward next waypoint, with optional pitch/roll/yaw offset. This can then be used by the vehicle's control system to control the vehicle attitude and the attitude of various sensors such as cameras. This command can be sent to a gimbal manager but not to a gimbal device. A gimbal device is not to react to this message. |Component ID of gimbal device to address (or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal), 0 for all gimbal device components. Send command multiple times for more than one gimbal (but not all gimbals).| Empty| Empty| Empty| Pitch offset from next waypoint, positive pitching up| Roll offset from next waypoint, positive rolling to the right| Yaw offset from next waypoint, positive yawing to the right| */
841 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_ROI_NONE=197, /* Cancels any previous ROI command returning the vehicle/sensors to default flight characteristics. This can then be used by the vehicle's control system to control the vehicle attitude and the attitude of various sensors such as cameras. This command can be sent to a gimbal manager but not to a gimbal device. A gimbal device is not to react to this message. After this command the gimbal manager should go back to manual input if available, and otherwise assume a neutral position. |Component ID of gimbal device to address (or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal), 0 for all gimbal device components. Send command multiple times for more than one gimbal (but not all gimbals).| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
842 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_ROI_SYSID=198, /* Mount tracks system with specified system ID. Determination of target vehicle position may be done with GLOBAL_POSITION_INT or any other means. This command can be sent to a gimbal manager but not to a gimbal device. A gimbal device is not to react to this message. |System ID| Component ID of gimbal device to address (or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal), 0 for all gimbal device components. Send command multiple times for more than one gimbal (but not all gimbals).| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
843 MAV_CMD_DO_CONTROL_VIDEO=200, /* Control onboard camera system. |Camera ID (-1 for all)| Transmission: 0: disabled, 1: enabled compressed, 2: enabled raw| Transmission mode: 0: video stream, >0: single images every n seconds| Recording: 0: disabled, 1: enabled compressed, 2: enabled raw| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
844 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_ROI=201, /* Sets the region of interest (ROI) for a sensor set or the vehicle itself. This can then be used by the vehicle's control system to control the vehicle attitude and the attitude of various sensors such as cameras. |Region of interest mode.| Waypoint index/ target ID (depends on param 1).| Region of interest index. (allows a vehicle to manage multiple ROI's)| Empty| MAV_ROI_WPNEXT: pitch offset from next waypoint, MAV_ROI_LOCATION: latitude| MAV_ROI_WPNEXT: roll offset from next waypoint, MAV_ROI_LOCATION: longitude| MAV_ROI_WPNEXT: yaw offset from next waypoint, MAV_ROI_LOCATION: altitude| */
845 MAV_CMD_DO_DIGICAM_CONFIGURE=202, /* Configure digital camera. This is a fallback message for systems that have not yet implemented PARAM_EXT_XXX messages and camera definition files (see https://mavlink.io/en/services/camera_def.html ). |Modes: P, TV, AV, M, Etc.| Shutter speed: Divisor number for one second.| Aperture: F stop number.| ISO number e.g. 80, 100, 200, Etc.| Exposure type enumerator.| Command Identity.| Main engine cut-off time before camera trigger. (0 means no cut-off)| */
846 MAV_CMD_DO_DIGICAM_CONTROL=203, /* Control digital camera. This is a fallback message for systems that have not yet implemented PARAM_EXT_XXX messages and camera definition files (see https://mavlink.io/en/services/camera_def.html ). |Session control e.g. show/hide lens| Zoom's absolute position| Zooming step value to offset zoom from the current position| Focus Locking, Unlocking or Re-locking| Shooting Command| Command Identity| Test shot identifier. If set to 1, image will only be captured, but not counted towards internal frame count.| */
847 MAV_CMD_DO_MOUNT_CONFIGURE=204, /* Mission command to configure a camera or antenna mount |Mount operation mode| Stabilize roll (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Stabilize pitch (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Stabilize yaw (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Roll input (0 = angle body frame, 1 = angular rate, 2 = angle absolute frame)| Pitch input (0 = angle body frame, 1 = angular rate, 2 = angle absolute frame)| Yaw input (0 = angle body frame, 1 = angular rate, 2 = angle absolute frame)| */
848 MAV_CMD_DO_MOUNT_CONTROL=205, /* Mission command to control a camera or antenna mount |pitch depending on mount mode (degrees or degrees/second depending on pitch input).| roll depending on mount mode (degrees or degrees/second depending on roll input).| yaw depending on mount mode (degrees or degrees/second depending on yaw input).| altitude depending on mount mode.| latitude, set if appropriate mount mode.| longitude, set if appropriate mount mode.| Mount mode.| */
849 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST=206, /* Mission command to set camera trigger distance for this flight. The camera is triggered each time this distance is exceeded. This command can also be used to set the shutter integration time for the camera. |Camera trigger distance. 0 to stop triggering.| Camera shutter integration time. -1 or 0 to ignore| Trigger camera once, immediately (MAV_BOOL_TRUE).
