Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
850 MAV_CMD_DO_FENCE_ENABLE=207, /*
851 Enable the geofence.
852 This can be used in a mission or via the command protocol.
853 The persistence/lifetime of the setting is undefined.
854 Depending on flight stack implementation it may persist until superseded, or it may revert to a system default at the end of a mission.
855 Flight stacks typically reset the setting to system defaults on reboot.
856 |enable? (0=disable, 1=enable, 2=disable_floor_only)| Fence types to enable or disable as a bitmask. 0: field is unused/all fences should be enabled or disabled (for compatibility reasons). Parameter is ignored if param1=2.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
857 MAV_CMD_DO_PARACHUTE=208, /* Mission item/command to release a parachute or enable/disable auto release. |Action| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
858 MAV_CMD_DO_MOTOR_TEST=209, /* Command to perform motor test. |Motor instance number (from 1 to max number of motors on the vehicle).| Throttle type (whether the Throttle Value in param3 is a percentage, PWM value, etc.)| Throttle value.| Timeout between tests that are run in sequence.| Motor count. Number of motors to test in sequence: 0/1=one motor, 2= two motors, etc. The Timeout (param4) is used between tests.| Motor test order.| Empty| */
859 MAV_CMD_DO_INVERTED_FLIGHT=210, /* Change to/from inverted flight. |Inverted flight (MAV_BOOL_False: normal flight). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
860 MAV_CMD_DO_GRIPPER=211, /* Mission command to operate a gripper. |Gripper ID. 1-6 for an autopilot connected gripper. In missions this may be set to 1-6 for an autopilot gripper, or the gripper component id for a MAVLink gripper. 0 targets all grippers.| Gripper action to perform.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
861 MAV_CMD_DO_AUTOTUNE_ENABLE=212, /* Enable/disable autotune. |Enable autotune (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: disable autotune). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Specify axes for which autotuning is enabled/disabled. 0 indicates the field is unused (for compatibility reasons). If 0 the autopilot will follow its default behaviour, which is usually to tune all axes.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| */
862 MAV_CMD_NAV_SET_YAW_SPEED=213, /* Sets a desired vehicle turn angle and speed change. |Yaw angle to adjust steering by.| Speed.| Relative final angle (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: Absolute angle). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
863 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_INTERVAL=214, /* Mission command to set camera trigger interval for this flight. If triggering is enabled, the camera is triggered each time this interval expires. This command can also be used to set the shutter integration time for the camera. |Camera trigger cycle time. -1 or 0 to ignore.| Camera shutter integration time. Should be less than trigger cycle time. -1 or 0 to ignore.| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
864 MAV_CMD_DO_MOUNT_CONTROL_QUAT=220, /* Mission command to control a camera or antenna mount, using a quaternion as reference. |quaternion param q1, w (1 in null-rotation)| quaternion param q2, x (0 in null-rotation)| quaternion param q3, y (0 in null-rotation)| quaternion param q4, z (0 in null-rotation)| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
865 MAV_CMD_DO_GUIDED_MASTER=221, /* set id of master controller |System ID| Component ID| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
866 MAV_CMD_DO_GUIDED_LIMITS=222, /* Set limits for external control |Timeout - maximum time that external controller will be allowed to control vehicle. 0 means no timeout.| Altitude (MSL) min - if vehicle moves below this alt, the command will be aborted and the mission will continue. 0 means no lower altitude limit.| Altitude (MSL) max - if vehicle moves above this alt, the command will be aborted and the mission will continue. 0 means no upper altitude limit.| Horizontal move limit - if vehicle moves more than this distance from its location at the moment the command was executed, the command will be aborted and the mission will continue. 0 means no horizontal move limit.| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
867 MAV_CMD_DO_ENGINE_CONTROL=223, /* Control vehicle engine. This is interpreted by the vehicles engine controller to change the target engine state. It is intended for vehicles with internal combustion engines |Start engine (MAV_BOOL_False: Stop engine). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Cold start engine (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: Warm start). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid. Controls use of choke where applicable| Height delay. This is for commanding engine start only after the vehicle has gained the specified height. Used in VTOL vehicles during takeoff to start engine after the aircraft is off the ground. Zero for no delay.| A bitmask of options for engine control| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
868 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_MISSION_CURRENT=224, /*
869 Set the mission item with sequence number seq as the current item and emit MISSION_CURRENT (whether or not the mission number changed).
870 If a mission is currently being executed, the system will continue to this new mission item on the shortest path, skipping any intermediate mission items.
871 Note that mission jump repeat counters are not reset unless param2 is set (see MAV_CMD_DO_JUMP param2).
872
873 This command may trigger a mission state-machine change on some systems: for example from MISSION_STATE_NOT_STARTED or MISSION_STATE_PAUSED to MISSION_STATE_ACTIVE.
874 If the system is in mission mode, on those systems this command might therefore start, restart or resume the mission.
875 If the system is not in mission mode this command must not trigger a switch to mission mode.
876
877 The mission may be "reset" using param2.
878 Resetting sets jump counters to initial values (to reset counters without changing the current mission item set the param1 to `-1`).
879 Resetting also explicitly changes a mission state of MISSION_STATE_COMPLETE to MISSION_STATE_PAUSED or MISSION_STATE_ACTIVE, potentially allowing it to resume when it is (next) in a mission mode.
880
881 The command will ACK with MAV_RESULT_FAILED if the sequence number is out of range (including if there is no mission item).
882 |Mission sequence value to set. -1 for the current mission item (use to reset mission without changing current mission item).| Reset mission (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid. Resets jump counters to initial values and changes mission state "completed" to be "active" or "paused".| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
883 MAV_CMD_DO_LAST=240, /* NOP - This command is only used to mark the upper limit of the DO commands in the enumeration |Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
884 MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_CALIBRATION=241, /* Trigger calibration. This command will be only accepted if in pre-flight mode. Except for Temperature Calibration, only one sensor should be set in a single message and all others should be zero. |1: gyro calibration, 3: gyro temperature calibration| Magnetometer calibration action.| Ground pressure calibration. Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| 1: radio RC calibration, 2: RC trim calibration| Accelerometer calibration action.| 1: APM: compass/motor interference calibration (PX4: airspeed calibration, deprecated), 2: airspeed calibration| 1: ESC calibration, 3: barometer temperature calibration| */
885 MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_SET_SENSOR_OFFSETS=242, /* Set sensor offsets. This command will be only accepted if in pre-flight mode. |Sensor to adjust the offsets for: 0: gyros, 1: accelerometer, 2: magnetometer, 3: barometer, 4: optical flow, 5: second magnetometer, 6: third magnetometer| X axis offset (or generic dimension 1), in the sensor's raw units| Y axis offset (or generic dimension 2), in the sensor's raw units| Z axis offset (or generic dimension 3), in the sensor's raw units| Generic dimension 4, in the sensor's raw units| Generic dimension 5, in the sensor's raw units| Generic dimension 6, in the sensor's raw units| */
886 MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_UAVCAN=243, /* Trigger UAVCAN configuration (actuator ID assignment and direction mapping). Note that this maps to the legacy UAVCAN v0 function UAVCAN_ENUMERATE, which is intended to be executed just once during initial vehicle configuration (it is not a normal pre-flight command and has been poorly named). |1: Trigger actuator ID assignment and direction mapping. 0: Cancel command.| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| */
887 MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_STORAGE=245, /* Request storage of different parameter values and logs. This command will be only accepted if in pre-flight mode. |Action to perform on the persistent parameter storage| Action to perform on the persistent mission storage| Onboard logging: 0: Ignore, 1: Start default rate logging, -1: Stop logging, > 1: logging rate (e.g. set to 1000 for 1000 Hz logging)| Reserved| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
888 MAV_CMD_PREFLIGHT_REBOOT_SHUTDOWN=246, /* Request the reboot or shutdown of system components. |Action to take for autopilot.| Action to take for onboard computer.| Action to take for component specified in param4.| MAVLink Component ID targeted in param3 (0 for all components).| Reserved (set to 0)| Conditions under which reboot/shutdown is allowed.| WIP: ID (e.g. camera ID -1 for all IDs)| */
889 MAV_CMD_OVERRIDE_GOTO=252, /* Override current mission with command to pause mission, pause mission and move to position, continue/resume mission. When param 1 indicates that the mission is paused (MAV_GOTO_DO_HOLD), param 2 defines whether it holds in place or moves to another position. |MAV_GOTO_DO_HOLD: pause mission and either hold or move to specified position (depending on param2), MAV_GOTO_DO_CONTINUE: resume mission.| MAV_GOTO_HOLD_AT_CURRENT_POSITION: hold at current position, MAV_GOTO_HOLD_AT_SPECIFIED_POSITION: hold at specified position.| Coordinate frame of hold point.| Desired yaw angle.| Latitude/X position.| Longitude/Y position.| Altitude/Z position.| */
890 MAV_CMD_OBLIQUE_SURVEY=260, /* Mission command to set a Camera Auto Mount Pivoting Oblique Survey (Replaces CAM_TRIGG_DIST for this purpose). The camera is triggered each time this distance is exceeded, then the mount moves to the next position. Params 4~6 set-up the angle limits and number of positions for oblique survey, where mount-enabled vehicles automatically roll the camera between shots to emulate an oblique camera setup (providing an increased HFOV). This command can also be used to set the shutter integration time for the camera. |Camera trigger distance. 0 to stop triggering.| Camera shutter integration time. 0 to ignore| The minimum interval in which the camera is capable of taking subsequent pictures repeatedly. 0 to ignore.| Total number of roll positions at which the camera will capture photos (images captures spread evenly across the limits defined by param5).| Angle limits that the camera can be rolled to left and right of center.| Fixed pitch angle that the camera will hold in oblique mode if the mount is actuated in the pitch axis.| Empty| */
891 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_STANDARD_MODE=262, /* Enable the specified standard MAVLink mode.
892 If the specified mode is not supported, the vehicle should ACK with MAV_RESULT_FAILED.
893 See https://mavlink.io/en/services/standard_modes.html
894 |The mode to set.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
895 MAV_CMD_MISSION_START=300, /* start running a mission |first_item: the first mission item to run| last_item: the last mission item to run (after this item is run, the mission ends)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
896 MAV_CMD_ACTUATOR_TEST=310, /* Actuator testing command. This is similar to MAV_CMD_DO_MOTOR_TEST but operates on the level of output functions, i.e. it is possible to test Motor1 independent from which output it is configured on. Autopilots must NACK this command with MAV_RESULT_TEMPORARILY_REJECTED while armed. |Output value: 1 means maximum positive output, 0 to center servos or minimum motor thrust (expected to spin), -1 for maximum negative (if not supported by the motors, i.e. motor is not reversible, smaller than 0 maps to NaN). And NaN maps to disarmed (stop the motors).| Timeout after which the test command expires and the output is restored to the previous value. A timeout has to be set for safety reasons. A timeout of 0 means to restore the previous value immediately.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Actuator Output function| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
897 MAV_CMD_CONFIGURE_ACTUATOR=311, /* Actuator configuration command. |Actuator configuration action| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Actuator Output function| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
898 MAV_CMD_COMPONENT_ARM_DISARM=400, /* Arms / Disarms a component |Arm (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: disarm). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| 0: arm-disarm unless prevented by safety checks (i.e. when landed), 21196: force arming/disarming (e.g. allow arming to override preflight checks and disarming in flight)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
899 MAV_CMD_RUN_PREARM_CHECKS=401, /* Instructs a target system to run pre-arm checks.
900 This allows preflight checks to be run on demand, which may be useful on systems that normally run them at low rate, or which do not trigger checks when the armable state might have changed.
901 This command should return MAV_RESULT_ACCEPTED if it will run the checks.
902 The results of the checks are usually then reported in SYS_STATUS messages (this is system-specific).
903 The command should return MAV_RESULT_TEMPORARILY_REJECTED if the system is already armed.
904 |Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
905 MAV_CMD_ILLUMINATOR_ON_OFF=405, /* Turns illuminators ON/OFF. An illuminator is a light source that is used for lighting up dark areas external to the system: e.g. a torch or searchlight (as opposed to a light source for illuminating the system itself, e.g. an indicator light). |Illuminators on/off (MAV_BOOL_TRUE: illuminators on). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
906 MAV_CMD_DO_ILLUMINATOR_CONFIGURE=406, /* Configures illuminator settings. An illuminator is a light source that is used for lighting up dark areas external to the system: e.g. a torch or searchlight (as opposed to a light source for illuminating the system itself, e.g. an indicator light). |Mode| 0%: Off, 100%: Max Brightness| Strobe period in seconds where 0 means strobing is not used| Strobe duty cycle where 100% means it is on constantly and 0 means strobing is not used| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
907 MAV_CMD_GET_HOME_POSITION=410, /* Request the home position from the vehicle.
908 The vehicle will ACK the command and emit the HOME_POSITION message. |Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| */
909 MAV_CMD_INJECT_FAILURE=420, /* Inject artificial failure for testing purposes. Note that autopilots should implement an additional protection before accepting this command such as a specific param setting. |The unit which is affected by the failure.| The type how the failure manifests itself.| Instance affected by failure (0 to signal all).| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
910 MAV_CMD_START_RX_PAIR=500, /* Starts receiver pairing. |RC type.| RC sub type.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
911 MAV_CMD_GET_MESSAGE_INTERVAL=510, /*
912 Request the interval between messages for a particular MAVLink message ID.
913 The receiver should ACK the command and then emit its response in a MESSAGE_INTERVAL message.
914 |The MAVLink message ID| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
915 MAV_CMD_SET_MESSAGE_INTERVAL=511, /* Set the interval between messages for a particular MAVLink message ID. This interface replaces REQUEST_DATA_STREAM. |The MAVLink message ID| The interval between two messages. -1: disable. 0: request default rate (which may be zero).| Use for index ID, if required. Otherwise, the use of this parameter (if any) must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0). When used as an index ID, 0 means "all instances", "1" means the first instance in the sequence (the emitted message will have an id of 0 if message ids are 0-indexed, or 1 if index numbers start from one).| The use of this parameter (if any), must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0).| The use of this parameter (if any), must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0/NaN).| The use of this parameter (if any), must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0/NaN).| Target address of message stream (if message has target address fields). 0: Flight-stack default (recommended), 1: address of requester, 2: broadcast.| */
916 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_MESSAGE=512, /* Request the target system(s) emit a single instance of a specified message (i.e. a "one-shot" version of MAV_CMD_SET_MESSAGE_INTERVAL). |The MAVLink message ID of the requested message.| Use for index ID, if required. Otherwise, the use of this parameter (if any) must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0).| The use of this parameter (if any), must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0).| The use of this parameter (if any), must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0).| The use of this parameter (if any), must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0).| The use of this parameter (if any), must be defined in the requested message. By default assumed not used (0).| Target address for requested message (if message has target address fields). 0: Flight-stack default, 1: address of requester, 2: broadcast.| */
917 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_PROTOCOL_VERSION=519, /* Request MAVLink protocol version compatibility. All receivers should ACK the command and then emit their capabilities in an PROTOCOL_VERSION message |Request supported protocol versions by all nodes on the network (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
918 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_AUTOPILOT_CAPABILITIES=520, /* Request autopilot capabilities. The receiver should ACK the command and then emit its capabilities in an AUTOPILOT_VERSION message |Request autopilot version (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
919 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_CAMERA_INFORMATION=521, /* Request camera information (CAMERA_INFORMATION). |Request camera capabilities (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
920 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_CAMERA_SETTINGS=522, /* Request camera settings (CAMERA_SETTINGS). |Request camera settings (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
921 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_STORAGE_INFORMATION=525, /* Request storage information (STORAGE_INFORMATION). Use the command's target_component to target a specific component's storage. |Storage ID (0 for all, 1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)| Request storage information (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
922 MAV_CMD_STORAGE_FORMAT=526, /* Format a storage medium. Once format is complete, a STORAGE_INFORMATION message is sent. Use the command's target_component to target a specific component's storage. |Storage ID (1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)| Format storage (and reset image log). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reset Image Log (without formatting storage medium). This will reset CAMERA_CAPTURE_STATUS.image_count and CAMERA_IMAGE_CAPTURED.image_index. Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
923 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_CAMERA_CAPTURE_STATUS=527, /* Request camera capture status (CAMERA_CAPTURE_STATUS) |Request camera capture status (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
924 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_FLIGHT_INFORMATION=528, /* Request flight information (FLIGHT_INFORMATION) |Request flight information (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Reserved (all remaining params)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
925 MAV_CMD_RESET_CAMERA_SETTINGS=529, /* Reset all camera settings to Factory Default |Reset all settings (MAV_BOOL_TRUE). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
926 MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_MODE=530, /* Set camera running mode. Use NaN for reserved values. GCS will send a MAV_CMD_REQUEST_VIDEO_STREAM_STATUS command after a mode change if the camera supports video streaming. |Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Camera mode| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
927 MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_ZOOM=531, /* Set camera zoom. Camera must respond with a CAMERA_SETTINGS message (on success). |Zoom type| Zoom value. The range of valid values depend on the zoom type.| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
928 MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_FOCUS=532, /* Set camera focus. Camera must respond with a CAMERA_SETTINGS message (on success). |Focus type| Focus value| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
929 MAV_CMD_SET_STORAGE_USAGE=533, /* Set that a particular storage is the preferred location for saving photos, videos, and/or other media (e.g. to set that an SD card is used for storing videos).
930 There can only be one preferred save location for each particular media type: setting a media usage flag will clear/reset that same flag if set on any other storage.
931 If no flag is set the system should use its default storage.
932 A target system can choose to always use default storage, in which case it should ACK the command with MAV_RESULT_UNSUPPORTED.
933 A target system can choose to not allow a particular storage to be set as preferred storage, in which case it should ACK the command with MAV_RESULT_DENIED. |Storage ID (1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)| Usage flags| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
934 MAV_CMD_SET_CAMERA_SOURCE=534, /* Set camera source. Changes the camera's active sources on cameras with multiple image sensors. |Component Id of camera to address or 1-6 for non-MAVLink cameras, 0 for all cameras.| Primary Source| Secondary Source. If non-zero the second source will be displayed as picture-in-picture.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
935 MAV_CMD_JUMP_TAG=600, /* Tagged jump target. Can be jumped to with MAV_CMD_DO_JUMP_TAG. |Tag.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
936 MAV_CMD_DO_JUMP_TAG=601, /* Jump to the matching tag in the mission list. Repeat this action for the specified number of times. A mission should contain a single matching tag for each jump. If this is not the case then a jump to a missing tag should complete the mission, and a jump where there are multiple matching tags should always select the one with the lowest mission sequence number. |Target tag to jump to.| Repeat count.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
937 MAV_CMD_DO_GIMBAL_MANAGER_PITCHYAW=1000, /* Set gimbal manager pitch/yaw setpoints (low rate command). It is possible to set combinations of the values below. E.g. an angle as well as a desired angular rate can be used to get to this angle at a certain angular rate, or an angular rate only will result in continuous turning. NaN is to be used to signal unset. Note: only the gimbal manager will react to this command - it will be ignored by a gimbal device. Use GIMBAL_MANAGER_SET_PITCHYAW if you need to stream pitch/yaw setpoints at higher rate. |Pitch angle (positive to pitch up, relative to vehicle for FOLLOW mode, relative to world horizon for LOCK mode).| Yaw angle (positive to yaw to the right, relative to vehicle for FOLLOW mode, absolute to North for LOCK mode).| Pitch rate (positive to pitch up).| Yaw rate (positive to yaw to the right).| Gimbal manager flags to use.| Reserved (default:0)| Component ID of gimbal device to address (or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal), 0 for all gimbal device components. Send command multiple times for more than one gimbal (but not all gimbals).| */
938 MAV_CMD_DO_GIMBAL_MANAGER_CONFIGURE=1001, /* Gimbal configuration to set which sysid/compid is in primary and secondary control. |Sysid for primary control (0: no one in control, -1: leave unchanged, -2: set itself in control (for missions where the own sysid is still unknown), -3: remove control if currently in control).| Compid for primary control (0: no one in control, -1: leave unchanged, -2: set itself in control (for missions where the own sysid is still unknown), -3: remove control if currently in control).| Sysid for secondary control (0: no one in control, -1: leave unchanged, -2: set itself in control (for missions where the own sysid is still unknown), -3: remove control if currently in control).| Compid for secondary control (0: no one in control, -1: leave unchanged, -2: set itself in control (for missions where the own sysid is still unknown), -3: remove control if currently in control).| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Component ID of gimbal device to address (or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal), 0 for all gimbal device components. Send command multiple times for more than one gimbal (but not all gimbals).| */
939 MAV_CMD_IMAGE_START_CAPTURE=2000, /* Start image capture sequence. CAMERA_IMAGE_CAPTURED must be emitted after each capture.
940
941 Param1 (id) may be used to specify the target camera: 0: all cameras, 1 to 6: autopilot-connected cameras, 7-255: MAVLink camera component ID.
942 It is needed in order to target specific cameras connected to the autopilot, or specific sensors in a multi-sensor camera (neither of which have a distinct MAVLink component ID).
943 It is also needed to specify the target camera in missions.
944
945 When used in a mission, an autopilot should execute the MAV_CMD for a specified local camera (param1 = 1-6), or resend it as a command if it is intended for a MAVLink camera (param1 = 7 - 255), setting the command's target_component as the param1 value (and setting param1 in the command to zero).
946 If the param1 is 0 the autopilot should do both.
947
948 When sent in a command the target MAVLink address is set using target_component.
949 If addressed specifically to an autopilot: param1 should be used in the same way as it is for missions (though command should NACK with MAV_RESULT_DENIED if a specified local camera does not exist).
950 If addressed to a MAVLink camera, param 1 can be used to address all cameras (0), or to separately address 1 to 7 individual sensors. Other values should be NACKed with MAV_RESULT_DENIED.
951 If the command is broadcast (target_component is 0) then param 1 should be set to 0 (any other value should be NACKED with MAV_RESULT_DENIED). An autopilot would trigger any local cameras and forward the command to all channels.
952 |Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Desired elapsed time between two consecutive pictures (in seconds). Minimum values depend on hardware (typically greater than 2 seconds).| Total number of images to capture. 0 to capture forever/until MAV_CMD_IMAGE_STOP_CAPTURE.| Capture sequence number starting from 1. This is only valid for single-capture (param3 == 1), otherwise set to 0. Increment the capture ID for each capture command to prevent double captures when a command is re-transmitted.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
953 MAV_CMD_IMAGE_STOP_CAPTURE=2001, /* Stop image capture sequence.
954
955 Param1 (id) may be used to specify the target camera: 0: all cameras, 1 to 6: autopilot-connected cameras, 7-255: MAVLink camera component ID.
956 It is needed in order to target specific cameras connected to the autopilot, or specific sensors in a multi-sensor camera (neither of which have a distinct MAVLink component ID).
957 It is also needed to specify the target camera in missions.
958
959 When used in a mission, an autopilot should execute the MAV_CMD for a specified local camera (param1 = 1-6), or resend it as a command if it is intended for a MAVLink camera (param1 = 7 - 255), setting the command's target_component as the param1 value (and setting param1 in the command to zero).
960 If the param1 is 0 the autopilot should do both.
961
962 When sent in a command the target MAVLink address is set using target_component.
963 If addressed specifically to an autopilot: param1 should be used in the same way as it is for missions (though command should NACK with MAV_RESULT_DENIED if a specified local camera does not exist).
964 If addressed to a MAVLink camera, param1 can be used to address all cameras (0), or to separately address 1 to 7 individual sensors. Other values should be NACKed with MAV_RESULT_DENIED.
965 If the command is broadcast (target_component is 0) then param 1 should be set to 0 (any other value should be NACKED with MAV_RESULT_DENIED). An autopilot would trigger any local cameras and forward the command to all channels.
966 |Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
967 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_CAMERA_IMAGE_CAPTURE=2002, /* Re-request a CAMERA_IMAGE_CAPTURED message. |Sequence number for missing CAMERA_IMAGE_CAPTURED message| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
968 MAV_CMD_DO_TRIGGER_CONTROL=2003, /* Enable or disable on-board camera triggering system. |Trigger enable/disable (0 for disable, 1 for start), -1 to ignore| 1 to reset the trigger sequence, -1 or 0 to ignore| 1 to pause triggering, but without switching the camera off or retracting it. -1 to ignore| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
969 MAV_CMD_CAMERA_TRACK_POINT=2004, /* If the camera supports point visual tracking (CAMERA_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_TRACKING_POINT is set), this command allows to initiate the tracking. |Point to track x value (normalized 0..1, 0 is left, 1 is right).| Point to track y value (normalized 0..1, 0 is top, 1 is bottom).| Point radius (normalized 0..1, 0 is one pixel, 1 is full image width).| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
970 MAV_CMD_CAMERA_TRACK_RECTANGLE=2005, /* If the camera supports rectangle visual tracking (CAMERA_CAP_FLAGS_HAS_TRACKING_RECTANGLE is set), this command allows to initiate the tracking. |Top left corner of rectangle x value (normalized 0..1, 0 is left, 1 is right).| Top left corner of rectangle y value (normalized 0..1, 0 is top, 1 is bottom).| Bottom right corner of rectangle x value (normalized 0..1, 0 is left, 1 is right).| Bottom right corner of rectangle y value (normalized 0..1, 0 is top, 1 is bottom).| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
971 MAV_CMD_CAMERA_STOP_TRACKING=2010, /* Stops ongoing tracking. |Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
972 MAV_CMD_VIDEO_START_CAPTURE=2500, /* Starts video capture (recording). |Video Stream ID (0 for all streams)| Frequency CAMERA_CAPTURE_STATUS messages should be sent while recording (0 for no messages, otherwise frequency)| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
973 MAV_CMD_VIDEO_STOP_CAPTURE=2501, /* Stop the current video capture (recording). |Video Stream ID (0 for all streams)| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
974 MAV_CMD_VIDEO_START_STREAMING=2502, /* Start video streaming |Video Stream ID (0 for all streams, 1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
975 MAV_CMD_VIDEO_STOP_STREAMING=2503, /* Stop the given video stream |Video Stream ID (0 for all streams, 1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)| Target camera ID. 7 to 255: MAVLink camera component id. 1 to 6 for cameras attached to the autopilot, which don't have a distinct component id. 0: all cameras. This is used to target specific autopilot-connected cameras. It is also used to target specific cameras when the MAV_CMD is used in a mission.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
976 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_VIDEO_STREAM_INFORMATION=2504, /* Request video stream information (VIDEO_STREAM_INFORMATION) |Video Stream ID (0 for all streams, 1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
977 MAV_CMD_REQUEST_VIDEO_STREAM_STATUS=2505, /* Request video stream status (VIDEO_STREAM_STATUS) |Video Stream ID (0 for all streams, 1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
978 MAV_CMD_LOGGING_START=2510, /* Request to start streaming logging data over MAVLink (see also LOGGING_DATA message) |Format: 0: ULog| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| */
979 MAV_CMD_LOGGING_STOP=2511, /* Request to stop streaming log data over MAVLink |Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| */
980 MAV_CMD_AIRFRAME_CONFIGURATION=2520, /* |Landing gear ID (default: 0, -1 for all)| Landing gear position (Down: 0, Up: 1, NaN for no change)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:NaN)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:NaN)| */
981 MAV_CMD_CONTROL_HIGH_LATENCY=2600, /* Request to start/stop transmitting over the high latency telemetry |Start transmission over high latency telemetry (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: stop transmission). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| Empty| */
982 MAV_CMD_PANORAMA_CREATE=2800, /* Create a panorama at the current position |Viewing angle horizontal of the panorama (+- 0.5 the total angle)| Viewing angle vertical of panorama.| Speed of the horizontal rotation.| Speed of the vertical rotation.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
983 MAV_CMD_DO_VTOL_TRANSITION=3000, /* Request VTOL transition |The target VTOL state. For normal transitions, only MAV_VTOL_STATE_MC and MAV_VTOL_STATE_FW can be used.| Force immediate transition to the specified MAV_VTOL_STATE. 1: Force immediate, 0: normal transition. Can be used, for example, to trigger an emergency "Quadchute". Caution: Can be dangerous/damage vehicle, depending on autopilot implementation of this command.| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
984 MAV_CMD_ARM_AUTHORIZATION_REQUEST=3001, /* Request authorization to arm the vehicle to a external entity, the arm authorizer is responsible to request all data that is needs from the vehicle before authorize or deny the request.
985 If approved the COMMAND_ACK message progress field should be set with period of time that this authorization is valid in seconds.
986 If the authorization is denied COMMAND_ACK.result_param2 should be set with one of the reasons in MAV_ARM_AUTH_DENIED_REASON.
987 |Vehicle system id, this way ground station can request arm authorization on behalf of any vehicle| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
988 MAV_CMD_SET_GUIDED_SUBMODE_STANDARD=4000, /* This command sets the submode to standard guided when vehicle is in guided mode. The vehicle holds position and altitude and the user can input the desired velocities along all three axes.
989 |Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| Reserved (default:0)| */
990 MAV_CMD_SET_GUIDED_SUBMODE_CIRCLE=4001, /* This command sets submode circle when vehicle is in guided mode. Vehicle flies along a circle facing the center of the circle. The user can input the velocity along the circle and change the radius. If no input is given the vehicle will hold position.
991 |Radius of desired circle in CIRCLE_MODE| User defined| User defined| User defined| Target latitude of center of circle in CIRCLE_MODE| Target longitude of center of circle in CIRCLE_MODE| Reserved (default:0)| */
992 MAV_CMD_CONDITION_GATE=4501, /* Delay mission state machine until gate has been reached. |Geometry: 0: orthogonal to path between previous and next waypoint.| Use altitude (MAV_BOOL_FALSE: ignore altitude). Values not equal to 0 or 1 are invalid.| Empty| Empty| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
993 MAV_CMD_NAV_FENCE_RETURN_POINT=5000, /* Fence return point (there can only be one such point in a geofence definition). If rally points are supported they should be used instead. |Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
994 MAV_CMD_NAV_FENCE_POLYGON_VERTEX_INCLUSION=5001, /* Fence vertex for an inclusion polygon (the polygon must not be self-intersecting). The vehicle must stay within this area. Minimum of 3 vertices required.
995 The vertices for a polygon must be sent sequentially, each with param1 set to the total number of vertices in the polygon.
996 |Polygon vertex count. This is the number of vertices in the current polygon (all vertices will have the same number).| Vehicle must be inside ALL inclusion zones in a single group, vehicle must be inside at least one group, must be the same for all points in each polygon| Reserved| Reserved| Latitude| Longitude| Reserved| */
997 MAV_CMD_NAV_FENCE_POLYGON_VERTEX_EXCLUSION=5002, /* Fence vertex for an exclusion polygon (the polygon must not be self-intersecting). The vehicle must stay outside this area. Minimum of 3 vertices required.
998 The vertices for a polygon must be sent sequentially, each with param1 set to the total number of vertices in the polygon.
999 |Polygon vertex count. This is the number of vertices in the current polygon (all vertices will have the same number).| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Latitude| Longitude| Reserved| */
1000 MAV_CMD_NAV_FENCE_CIRCLE_INCLUSION=5003, /* Circular fence area. The vehicle must stay inside this area.
1001 |Radius.| Vehicle must be inside ALL inclusion zones in a single group, vehicle must be inside at least one group| Reserved| Reserved| Latitude| Longitude| Reserved| */
1002 MAV_CMD_NAV_FENCE_CIRCLE_EXCLUSION=5004, /* Circular fence area. The vehicle must stay outside this area.
1003 |Radius.| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Latitude| Longitude| Reserved| */
1004 MAV_CMD_NAV_RALLY_POINT=5100, /* Rally point. You can have multiple rally points defined.
1005 |Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude| */
1006 MAV_CMD_UAVCAN_GET_NODE_INFO=5200, /* Commands the vehicle to respond with a sequence of messages UAVCAN_NODE_INFO, one message per every UAVCAN node that is online. Note that some of the response messages can be lost, which the receiver can detect easily by checking whether every received UAVCAN_NODE_STATUS has a matching message UAVCAN_NODE_INFO received earlier; if not, this command should be sent again in order to request re-transmission of the node information messages. |Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| */
1007 MAV_CMD_DO_SET_SAFETY_SWITCH_STATE=5300, /* Change state of safety switch. |New safety switch state.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| */
1008 MAV_CMD_DO_ADSB_OUT_IDENT=10001, /* Trigger the start of an ADSB-out IDENT. This should only be used when requested to do so by an Air Traffic Controller in controlled airspace. This starts the IDENT which is then typically held for 18 seconds by the hardware per the Mode A, C, and S transponder spec. |Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| Reserved (set to 0)| */
1009 MAV_CMD_PAYLOAD_PREPARE_DEPLOY=30001, /* Deploy payload on a Lat / Lon / Alt position. This includes the navigation to reach the required release position and velocity. |Operation mode. 0: prepare single payload deploy (overwriting previous requests), but do not execute it. 1: execute payload deploy immediately (rejecting further deploy commands during execution, but allowing abort). 2: add payload deploy to existing deployment list.| Desired approach vector in compass heading. A negative value indicates the system can define the approach vector at will.| Desired ground speed at release time. This can be overridden by the airframe in case it needs to meet minimum airspeed. A negative value indicates the system can define the ground speed at will.| Minimum altitude clearance to the release position. A negative value indicates the system can define the clearance at will.| Latitude.| Longitude.| Altitude (MSL)| */
1010 MAV_CMD_PAYLOAD_CONTROL_DEPLOY=30002, /* Control the payload deployment. |Operation mode. 0: Abort deployment, continue normal mission. 1: switch to payload deployment mode. 100: delete first payload deployment request. 101: delete all payload deployment requests.| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| Reserved| */
1011 MAV_CMD_WAYPOINT_USER_1=31000, /* User defined waypoint item. Ground Station will show the Vehicle as flying through this item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1012 MAV_CMD_WAYPOINT_USER_2=31001, /* User defined waypoint item. Ground Station will show the Vehicle as flying through this item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1013 MAV_CMD_WAYPOINT_USER_3=31002, /* User defined waypoint item. Ground Station will show the Vehicle as flying through this item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1014 MAV_CMD_WAYPOINT_USER_4=31003, /* User defined waypoint item. Ground Station will show the Vehicle as flying through this item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1015 MAV_CMD_WAYPOINT_USER_5=31004, /* User defined waypoint item. Ground Station will show the Vehicle as flying through this item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1016 MAV_CMD_SPATIAL_USER_1=31005, /* User defined spatial item. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: ROI item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1017 MAV_CMD_SPATIAL_USER_2=31006, /* User defined spatial item. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: ROI item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1018 MAV_CMD_SPATIAL_USER_3=31007, /* User defined spatial item. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: ROI item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1019 MAV_CMD_SPATIAL_USER_4=31008, /* User defined spatial item. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: ROI item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1020 MAV_CMD_SPATIAL_USER_5=31009, /* User defined spatial item. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: ROI item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| Latitude unscaled| Longitude unscaled| Altitude (MSL)| */
1021 MAV_CMD_USER_1=31010, /* User defined command. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: MAV_CMD_DO_SET_PARAMETER item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| */
1022 MAV_CMD_USER_2=31011, /* User defined command. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: MAV_CMD_DO_SET_PARAMETER item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| */
1023 MAV_CMD_USER_3=31012, /* User defined command. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: MAV_CMD_DO_SET_PARAMETER item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| */
1024 MAV_CMD_USER_4=31013, /* User defined command. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: MAV_CMD_DO_SET_PARAMETER item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| */
1025 MAV_CMD_USER_5=31014, /* User defined command. Ground Station will not show the Vehicle as flying through this item. Example: MAV_CMD_DO_SET_PARAMETER item. |User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| User defined| */
1026 MAV_CMD_CAN_FORWARD=32000, /* Request forwarding of CAN packets from the given CAN bus to this component via this MAVLink channel. CAN Frames are sent using CAN_FRAME and CANFD_FRAME messages |Bus number (0 to disable forwarding, 1 for first bus, 2 for 2nd bus, 3 for 3rd bus).| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| */
1027 MAV_CMD_FIXED_MAG_CAL_YAW=42006, /* Magnetometer calibration based on provided known yaw. This allows for fast calibration using WMM field tables in the vehicle, given only the known yaw of the vehicle. If Latitude and longitude are both zero then use the current vehicle location. |Yaw of vehicle in earth frame.| CompassMask, 0 for all.| Latitude.| Longitude.| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| */
1028 MAV_CMD_DO_WINCH=42600, /* Command to operate winch. |Winch instance number.| Action to perform.| Length of line to release (negative to wind).| Release rate (negative to wind).| Empty.| Empty.| Empty.| */
1029 MAV_CMD_EXTERNAL_POSITION_ESTIMATE=43003, /* Provide an external position estimate for use when dead-reckoning. This is meant to be used for occasional position resets that may be provided by a external system such as a remote pilot using landmarks over a video link. |Timestamp that this message was sent as a time in the transmitters time domain. The sender should wrap this time back to zero based on required timing accuracy for the application and the limitations of a 32 bit float. For example, wrapping at 10 hours would give approximately 1ms accuracy. Recipient must handle time wrap in any timing jitter correction applied to this field. Wrap rollover time should not be at not more than 250 seconds, which would give approximately 10 microsecond accuracy.| The time spent in processing the sensor data that is the basis for this position. The recipient can use this to improve time alignment of the data. Set to zero if not known.| estimated one standard deviation accuracy of the measurement. Set to NaN if not known.| Empty| Latitude| Longitude| Altitude, not used. Should be sent as NaN. May be supported in a future version of this message.| */
1030 MAV_CMD_ENUM_END=43004, /* | */
1031} MAV_CMD;
1032#endif
1033
1034/** @brief A data stream is not a fixed set of messages, but rather a
1035 recommendation to the autopilot software. Individual autopilots may or may not obey
1036 the recommended messages. */
1037#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DATA_STREAM
1038#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DATA_STREAM
1039typedef enum MAV_DATA_STREAM
1040{
1041 MAV_DATA_STREAM_ALL=0, /* Enable all data streams | */
1042 MAV_DATA_STREAM_RAW_SENSORS=1, /* Enable IMU_RAW, GPS_RAW, GPS_STATUS packets. | */
1043 MAV_DATA_STREAM_EXTENDED_STATUS=2, /* Enable GPS_STATUS, CONTROL_STATUS, AUX_STATUS | */
1044 MAV_DATA_STREAM_RC_CHANNELS=3, /* Enable RC_CHANNELS_SCALED, RC_CHANNELS_RAW, SERVO_OUTPUT_RAW | */
1045 MAV_DATA_STREAM_RAW_CONTROLLER=4, /* Enable ATTITUDE_CONTROLLER_OUTPUT, POSITION_CONTROLLER_OUTPUT, NAV_CONTROLLER_OUTPUT. | */
1046 MAV_DATA_STREAM_POSITION=6, /* Enable LOCAL_POSITION, GLOBAL_POSITION_INT messages. | */
1047 MAV_DATA_STREAM_EXTRA1=10, /* Dependent on the autopilot | */
1048 MAV_DATA_STREAM_EXTRA2=11, /* Dependent on the autopilot | */
1049 MAV_DATA_STREAM_EXTRA3=12, /* Dependent on the autopilot | */
1050 MAV_DATA_STREAM_ENUM_END=13, /* | */
1051} MAV_DATA_STREAM;
1052#endif
1053
1054/** @brief The ROI (region of interest) for the vehicle. This can be
1055 be used by the vehicle for camera/vehicle attitude alignment (see
1056 MAV_CMD_NAV_ROI). */
1057#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ROI
1058#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ROI
1059typedef enum MAV_ROI
1060{
1061 MAV_ROI_NONE=0, /* No region of interest. | */
1062 MAV_ROI_WPNEXT=1, /* Point toward next waypoint, with optional pitch/roll/yaw offset. | */
1063 MAV_ROI_WPINDEX=2, /* Point toward given waypoint. | */
1064 MAV_ROI_LOCATION=3, /* Point toward fixed location. | */
1065 MAV_ROI_TARGET=4, /* Point toward of given id. | */
1066 MAV_ROI_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
1067} MAV_ROI;
1068#endif
1069
1070/** @brief Specifies the datatype of a MAVLink parameter. */
1071#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_TYPE
1072#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_TYPE
1073typedef enum MAV_PARAM_TYPE
1074{
1075 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_UINT8=1, /* 8-bit unsigned integer | */
1076 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_INT8=2, /* 8-bit signed integer | */
1077 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_UINT16=3, /* 16-bit unsigned integer | */
1078 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_INT16=4, /* 16-bit signed integer | */
1079 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_UINT32=5, /* 32-bit unsigned integer | */
1080 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_INT32=6, /* 32-bit signed integer | */
1081 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_UINT64=7, /* 64-bit unsigned integer | */
1082 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_INT64=8, /* 64-bit signed integer | */
1083 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_REAL32=9, /* 32-bit floating-point | */
1084 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_REAL64=10, /* 64-bit floating-point | */
1085 MAV_PARAM_TYPE_ENUM_END=11, /* | */
1086} MAV_PARAM_TYPE;
1087#endif
1088
1089/** @brief Parameter protocol error types (see PARAM_ERROR). */
1090#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_ERROR
1091#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_ERROR
1092typedef enum MAV_PARAM_ERROR
1093{
1094 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_NO_ERROR=0, /* No error occurred (not expected in PARAM_ERROR but may be used in future implementations. | */
1095 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_DOES_NOT_EXIST=1, /* Parameter does not exist | */
1096 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE=2, /* Parameter value does not fit within accepted range | */
1097 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_PERMISSION_DENIED=3, /* Caller is not permitted to set the value of this parameter | */
1098 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_COMPONENT_NOT_FOUND=4, /* Unknown component specified | */
1099 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_READ_ONLY=5, /* Parameter is read-only | */
1100 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_TYPE_UNSUPPORTED=6, /* Parameter data type (MAV_PARAM_TYPE) is not supported by flight stack (at all) | */
1101 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_TYPE_MISMATCH=7, /* Parameter type does not match expected type | */
1102 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_READ_FAIL=8, /* Parameter exists but reading failed | */
1103 MAV_PARAM_ERROR_ENUM_END=9, /* | */
1104} MAV_PARAM_ERROR;
1105#endif
1106
1107/** @brief Specifies the datatype of a MAVLink extended parameter. */
1108#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE
1109#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE
1110typedef enum MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE
1111{
1112 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_UINT8=1, /* 8-bit unsigned integer | */
1113 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_INT8=2, /* 8-bit signed integer | */
1114 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_UINT16=3, /* 16-bit unsigned integer | */
1115 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_INT16=4, /* 16-bit signed integer | */
1116 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_UINT32=5, /* 32-bit unsigned integer | */
1117 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_INT32=6, /* 32-bit signed integer | */
1118 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_UINT64=7, /* 64-bit unsigned integer | */
1119 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_INT64=8, /* 64-bit signed integer | */
1120 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_REAL32=9, /* 32-bit floating-point | */
1121 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_REAL64=10, /* 64-bit floating-point | */
1122 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_CUSTOM=11, /* Custom Type | */
1123 MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE_ENUM_END=12, /* | */
1124} MAV_PARAM_EXT_TYPE;
1125#endif
1126
1127/** @brief Result from a MAVLink command (MAV_CMD) */
1128#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_RESULT
1129#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_RESULT
1130typedef enum MAV_RESULT
1131{
1132 MAV_RESULT_ACCEPTED=0, /* Command is valid (is supported and has valid parameters), and was executed. | */
1133 MAV_RESULT_TEMPORARILY_REJECTED=1, /* Command is valid, but cannot be executed at this time. This is used to indicate a problem that should be fixed just by waiting (e.g. a state machine is busy, can't arm because have not got GPS lock, etc.). Retrying later should work. | */
1134 MAV_RESULT_DENIED=2, /* Command is invalid; it is supported but one or more parameter values are invalid (i.e. parameter reserved, value allowed by spec but not supported by flight stack, and so on). Retrying the same command and parameters will not work. | */
1135 MAV_RESULT_UNSUPPORTED=3, /* Command is not supported (unknown). | */
1136 MAV_RESULT_FAILED=4, /* Command is valid, but execution has failed. This is used to indicate any non-temporary or unexpected problem, i.e. any problem that must be fixed before the command can succeed/be retried. For example, attempting to write a file when out of memory, attempting to arm when sensors are not calibrated, etc. | */
1137 MAV_RESULT_IN_PROGRESS=5, /* Command is valid and is being executed. This will be followed by further progress updates, i.e. the component may send further COMMAND_ACK messages with result MAV_RESULT_IN_PROGRESS (at a rate decided by the implementation), and must terminate by sending a COMMAND_ACK message with final result of the operation. The COMMAND_ACK.progress field can be used to indicate the progress of the operation. | */
1138 MAV_RESULT_CANCELLED=6, /* Command has been cancelled (as a result of receiving a COMMAND_CANCEL message). | */
1139 MAV_RESULT_COMMAND_LONG_ONLY=7, /* Command is only accepted when sent as a COMMAND_LONG. | */
1140 MAV_RESULT_COMMAND_INT_ONLY=8, /* Command is only accepted when sent as a COMMAND_INT. | */
1141 MAV_RESULT_COMMAND_UNSUPPORTED_MAV_FRAME=9, /* Command is invalid because a frame is required and the specified frame is not supported. | */
1142 MAV_RESULT_NOT_IN_CONTROL=10, /* Command has been rejected because source system is not in control of the target system/component. | */
1143 MAV_RESULT_ENUM_END=11, /* | */
1144} MAV_RESULT;
1145#endif
1146
1147/** @brief Result of mission operation (in a MISSION_ACK message). */
1148#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_RESULT
1149#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_RESULT
1150typedef enum MAV_MISSION_RESULT
1151{
1152 MAV_MISSION_ACCEPTED=0, /* mission accepted OK | */
1153 MAV_MISSION_ERROR=1, /* Generic error / not accepting mission commands at all right now. | */
1154 MAV_MISSION_UNSUPPORTED_FRAME=2, /* Coordinate frame is not supported. | */
1155 MAV_MISSION_UNSUPPORTED=3, /* Command is not supported. | */
1156 MAV_MISSION_NO_SPACE=4, /* Mission items exceed storage space. | */
1157 MAV_MISSION_INVALID=5, /* One of the parameters has an invalid value. | */
1158 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_PARAM1=6, /* param1 has an invalid value. | */
1159 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_PARAM2=7, /* param2 has an invalid value. | */
1160 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_PARAM3=8, /* param3 has an invalid value. | */
1161 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_PARAM4=9, /* param4 has an invalid value. | */
1162 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_PARAM5_X=10, /* x / param5 has an invalid value. | */
1163 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_PARAM6_Y=11, /* y / param6 has an invalid value. | */
1164 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_PARAM7=12, /* z / param7 has an invalid value. | */
1165 MAV_MISSION_INVALID_SEQUENCE=13, /* Mission item received out of sequence | */
1166 MAV_MISSION_DENIED=14, /* Not accepting any mission commands from this communication partner. | */
1167 MAV_MISSION_OPERATION_CANCELLED=15, /* Current mission operation cancelled (e.g. mission upload, mission download). | */
1168 MAV_MISSION_RESULT_ENUM_END=16, /* | */
1169} MAV_MISSION_RESULT;
1170#endif
1171
1172/** @brief Indicates the severity level, generally used for status messages to indicate their relative urgency. Based on RFC-5424 using expanded definitions at: http://www.kiwisyslog.com/kb/info:-syslog-message-levels/. */
1173#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SEVERITY
1174#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SEVERITY
1175typedef enum MAV_SEVERITY
1176{
1177 MAV_SEVERITY_EMERGENCY=0, /* System is unusable. This is a "panic" condition. | */
1178 MAV_SEVERITY_ALERT=1, /* Action should be taken immediately. Indicates error in non-critical systems. | */
1179 MAV_SEVERITY_CRITICAL=2, /* Action must be taken immediately. Indicates failure in a primary system. | */
1180 MAV_SEVERITY_ERROR=3, /* Indicates an error in secondary/redundant systems. | */
1181 MAV_SEVERITY_WARNING=4, /* Indicates about a possible future error if this is not resolved within a given timeframe. Example would be a low battery warning. | */
1182 MAV_SEVERITY_NOTICE=5, /* An unusual event has occurred, though not an error condition. This should be investigated for the root cause. | */
1183 MAV_SEVERITY_INFO=6, /* Normal operational messages. Useful for logging. No action is required for these messages. | */
1184 MAV_SEVERITY_DEBUG=7, /* Useful non-operational messages that can assist in debugging. These should not occur during normal operation. | */
1185 MAV_SEVERITY_ENUM_END=8, /* | */
1186} MAV_SEVERITY;
1187#endif
1188
1189/** @brief Power supply status flags (bitmask) */
1190#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_POWER_STATUS
1191#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_POWER_STATUS
1192typedef enum MAV_POWER_STATUS
1193{
1194 MAV_POWER_STATUS_BRICK_VALID=1, /* main brick power supply valid | */
1195 MAV_POWER_STATUS_SERVO_VALID=2, /* main servo power supply valid for FMU | */
1196 MAV_POWER_STATUS_USB_CONNECTED=4, /* USB power is connected | */
1197 MAV_POWER_STATUS_PERIPH_OVERCURRENT=8, /* peripheral supply is in over-current state | */
1198 MAV_POWER_STATUS_PERIPH_HIPOWER_OVERCURRENT=16, /* hi-power peripheral supply is in over-current state | */
1199 MAV_POWER_STATUS_CHANGED=32, /* Power status has changed since boot | */
1200 MAV_POWER_STATUS_ENUM_END=33, /* | */
1201} MAV_POWER_STATUS;
1202#endif
1203
1204/** @brief SERIAL_CONTROL device types */
1205#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV
1206#define HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV
1207typedef enum SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV
1208{
1209 SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV_TELEM1=0, /* First telemetry port | */
1210 SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV_TELEM2=1, /* Second telemetry port | */
1211 SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV_GPS1=2, /* First GPS port | */
1212 SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV_GPS2=3, /* Second GPS port | */
1213 SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV_SHELL=10, /* system shell | */
1214 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL0=100, /* SERIAL0 | */
1215 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL1=101, /* SERIAL1 | */
1216 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL2=102, /* SERIAL2 | */
1217 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL3=103, /* SERIAL3 | */
1218 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL4=104, /* SERIAL4 | */
1219 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL5=105, /* SERIAL5 | */
1220 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL6=106, /* SERIAL6 | */
1221 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL7=107, /* SERIAL7 | */
1222 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL8=108, /* SERIAL8 | */
1223 SERIAL_CONTROL_SERIAL9=109, /* SERIAL9 | */
1224 SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV_ENUM_END=110, /* | */
1225} SERIAL_CONTROL_DEV;
1226#endif
1227
1228/** @brief SERIAL_CONTROL flags (bitmask) */
1229#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG
1230#define HAVE_ENUM_SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG
1231typedef enum SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG
1232{
1233 SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG_REPLY=1, /* Set if this is a reply | */
1234 SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG_RESPOND=2, /* Set if the sender wants the receiver to send a response as another SERIAL_CONTROL message | */
1235 SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG_EXCLUSIVE=4, /* Set if access to the serial port should be removed from whatever driver is currently using it, giving exclusive access to the SERIAL_CONTROL protocol. The port can be handed back by sending a request without this flag set | */
1236 SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG_BLOCKING=8, /* Block on writes to the serial port | */
1237 SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG_MULTI=16, /* Send multiple replies until port is drained | */
1238 SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG_ENUM_END=17, /* | */
1239} SERIAL_CONTROL_FLAG;
1240#endif
1241
1242/** @brief Enumeration of distance sensor types */
1243#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR
1244#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR
1245typedef enum MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR
1246{
1247 MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR_LASER=0, /* Laser rangefinder, e.g. LightWare SF02/F or PulsedLight units | */
1248 MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR_ULTRASOUND=1, /* Ultrasound rangefinder, e.g. MaxBotix units | */
1249 MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR_INFRARED=2, /* Infrared rangefinder, e.g. Sharp units | */
1250 MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR_RADAR=3, /* Radar type, e.g. uLanding units | */
1251 MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR_UNKNOWN=4, /* Broken or unknown type, e.g. analog units | */
1252 MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
1253} MAV_DISTANCE_SENSOR;
1254#endif
1255
1256/** @brief Enumeration of sensor orientation, according to its rotations */
1257#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION
1258#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION
1259typedef enum MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION
1260{
1261 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_NONE=0, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 0 | */
1262 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_YAW_45=1, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 45 | */
1263 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_YAW_90=2, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 90 | */
1264 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_YAW_135=3, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 135 | */
1265 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_YAW_180=4, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 180 | */
1266 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_YAW_225=5, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 225 | */
1267 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_YAW_270=6, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 270 | */
1268 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_YAW_315=7, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 315 | */
1269 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180=8, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 0 | */
1270 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_YAW_45=9, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 45 | */
1271 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_YAW_90=10, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 90 | */
1272 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_YAW_135=11, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 135 | */
1273 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_PITCH_180=12, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 180, Yaw: 0 | */
1274 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_YAW_225=13, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 225 | */
1275 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_YAW_270=14, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 270 | */
1276 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_YAW_315=15, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 315 | */
1277 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90=16, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 0 | */
1278 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_YAW_45=17, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 45 | */
1279 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_YAW_90=18, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 90 | */
1280 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_YAW_135=19, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 135 | */
1281 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_270=20, /* Roll: 270, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 0 | */
1282 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_270_YAW_45=21, /* Roll: 270, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 45 | */
1283 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_270_YAW_90=22, /* Roll: 270, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 90 | */
1284 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_270_YAW_135=23, /* Roll: 270, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 135 | */
1285 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_PITCH_90=24, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 90, Yaw: 0 | */
1286 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_PITCH_270=25, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 270, Yaw: 0 | */
1287 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_PITCH_180_YAW_90=26, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 180, Yaw: 90 | */
1288 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_PITCH_180_YAW_270=27, /* Roll: 0, Pitch: 180, Yaw: 270 | */
1289 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_PITCH_90=28, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 90, Yaw: 0 | */
1290 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_PITCH_90=29, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 90, Yaw: 0 | */
1291 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_270_PITCH_90=30, /* Roll: 270, Pitch: 90, Yaw: 0 | */
1292 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_PITCH_180=31, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 180, Yaw: 0 | */
1293 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_270_PITCH_180=32, /* Roll: 270, Pitch: 180, Yaw: 0 | */
1294 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_PITCH_270=33, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 270, Yaw: 0 | */
1295 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_180_PITCH_270=34, /* Roll: 180, Pitch: 270, Yaw: 0 | */
1296 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_270_PITCH_270=35, /* Roll: 270, Pitch: 270, Yaw: 0 | */
1297 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_PITCH_180_YAW_90=36, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 180, Yaw: 90 | */
1298 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_YAW_270=37, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 0, Yaw: 270 | */
1299 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_PITCH_68_YAW_293=38, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 68, Yaw: 293 | */
1300 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_PITCH_315=39, /* Pitch: 315 | */
1301 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_ROLL_90_PITCH_315=40, /* Roll: 90, Pitch: 315 | */
1302 MAV_SENSOR_ROTATION_CUSTOM=100, /* Custom orientation | */
1303 MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION_ENUM_END=101, /* | */
1304} MAV_SENSOR_ORIENTATION;
1305#endif
1306
1307/** @brief Type of mission items being requested/sent in mission protocol. */
1308#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_TYPE
1309#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_MISSION_TYPE
1310typedef enum MAV_MISSION_TYPE
1311{
1312 MAV_MISSION_TYPE_MISSION=0, /* Items are mission commands for main mission. | */
1313 MAV_MISSION_TYPE_FENCE=1, /* Specifies GeoFence area(s). Items are MAV_CMD_NAV_FENCE_ GeoFence items. | */
1314 MAV_MISSION_TYPE_RALLY=2, /* Specifies the rally points for the vehicle. Rally points are alternative RTL points. Items are MAV_CMD_NAV_RALLY_POINT rally point items. | */
1315 MAV_MISSION_TYPE_ALL=255, /* Only used in MISSION_CLEAR_ALL to clear all mission types. | */
1316 MAV_MISSION_TYPE_ENUM_END=256, /* | */
1317} MAV_MISSION_TYPE;
1318#endif
1319
1320/** @brief Enumeration of estimator types */
1321#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE
1322#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE
1323typedef enum MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE
1324{
1325 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_UNKNOWN=0, /* Unknown type of the estimator. | */
1326 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_NAIVE=1, /* This is a naive estimator without any real covariance feedback. | */
1327 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_VISION=2, /* Computer vision based estimate. Might be up to scale. | */
1328 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_VIO=3, /* Visual-inertial estimate. | */
1329 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_GPS=4, /* Plain GPS estimate. | */
1330 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_GPS_INS=5, /* Estimator integrating GPS and inertial sensing. | */
1331 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_MOCAP=6, /* Estimate from external motion capturing system. | */
1332 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_LIDAR=7, /* Estimator based on lidar sensor input. | */
1333 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_AUTOPILOT=8, /* Estimator on autopilot. | */
1334 MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE_ENUM_END=9, /* | */
1335} MAV_ESTIMATOR_TYPE;
1336#endif
1337
1338/** @brief Enumeration of battery types */
1339#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_TYPE
1340#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_TYPE
1341typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_TYPE
1342{
1343 MAV_BATTERY_TYPE_UNKNOWN=0, /* Not specified. | */
1344 MAV_BATTERY_TYPE_LIPO=1, /* Lithium polymer battery | */
1345 MAV_BATTERY_TYPE_LIFE=2, /* Lithium-iron-phosphate battery | */
1346 MAV_BATTERY_TYPE_LION=3, /* Lithium-ION battery | */
1347 MAV_BATTERY_TYPE_NIMH=4, /* Nickel metal hydride battery | */
1348 MAV_BATTERY_TYPE_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
1349} MAV_BATTERY_TYPE;
1350#endif
1351
1352/** @brief Enumeration of battery functions */
1353#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION
1354#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION
1355typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION
1356{
1357 MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION_UNKNOWN=0, /* Battery function is unknown | */
1358 MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION_ALL=1, /* Battery supports all flight systems | */
1359 MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION_PROPULSION=2, /* Battery for the propulsion system | */
1360 MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION_AVIONICS=3, /* Avionics battery | */
1361 MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION_PAYLOAD=4, /* Payload battery | */
1362 MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION_ENUM_END=5, /* | */
1363} MAV_BATTERY_FUNCTION;
1364#endif
1365
1366/** @brief Enumeration for battery charge states. */
1367#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE
1368#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE
1369typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE
1370{
1371 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_UNDEFINED=0, /* Low battery state is not provided | */
1372 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_OK=1, /* Battery is not in low state. Normal operation. | */
1373 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_LOW=2, /* Battery state is low, warn and monitor close. | */
1374 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_CRITICAL=3, /* Battery state is critical, return or abort immediately. | */
1375 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_EMERGENCY=4, /* Battery state is too low for ordinary abort sequence. Perform fastest possible emergency stop to prevent damage. | */
1376 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_FAILED=5, /* Battery failed, damage unavoidable. Possible causes (faults) are listed in MAV_BATTERY_FAULT. | */
1377 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_UNHEALTHY=6, /* Battery is diagnosed to be defective or an error occurred, usage is discouraged / prohibited. Possible causes (faults) are listed in MAV_BATTERY_FAULT. | */
1378 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_CHARGING=7, /* Battery is charging. | */
1379 MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_ENUM_END=8, /* | */
1380} MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE;
1381#endif
1382
1383/** @brief Battery mode. Note, the normal operation mode (i.e. when flying) should be reported as MAV_BATTERY_MODE_UNKNOWN to allow message trimming in normal flight. */
1384#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_MODE
1385#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_MODE
1386typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_MODE
1387{
1388 MAV_BATTERY_MODE_UNKNOWN=0, /* Battery mode not supported/unknown battery mode/normal operation. | */
1389 MAV_BATTERY_MODE_AUTO_DISCHARGING=1, /* Battery is auto discharging (towards storage level). | */
1390 MAV_BATTERY_MODE_HOT_SWAP=2, /* Battery in hot-swap mode (current limited to prevent spikes that might damage sensitive electrical circuits). | */
1391 MAV_BATTERY_MODE_ENUM_END=3, /* | */
1392} MAV_BATTERY_MODE;
1393#endif
1394
1395/** @brief Smart battery supply status/fault flags (bitmask) for health indication. The battery must also report either MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_FAILED or MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_UNHEALTHY if any of these are set. */
1396#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FAULT
1397#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_BATTERY_FAULT
1398typedef enum MAV_BATTERY_FAULT
1399{
1400 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_DEEP_DISCHARGE=1, /* Battery has deep discharged. | */
1401 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_SPIKES=2, /* Voltage spikes. | */
1402 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_CELL_FAIL=4, /* One or more cells have failed. Battery should also report MAV_BATTERY_CHARGE_STATE_FAILE (and should not be used). | */
1403 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_OVER_CURRENT=8, /* Over-current fault. | */
1404 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_OVER_TEMPERATURE=16, /* Over-temperature fault. | */
1405 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_UNDER_TEMPERATURE=32, /* Under-temperature fault. | */
1406 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_INCOMPATIBLE_VOLTAGE=64, /* Vehicle voltage is not compatible with this battery (batteries on same power rail should have similar voltage). | */
1407 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_INCOMPATIBLE_FIRMWARE=128, /* Battery firmware is not compatible with current autopilot firmware. | */
1408 BATTERY_FAULT_INCOMPATIBLE_CELLS_CONFIGURATION=256, /* Battery is not compatible due to cell configuration (e.g. 5s1p when vehicle requires 6s). | */
1409 MAV_BATTERY_FAULT_ENUM_END=257, /* | */
1410} MAV_BATTERY_FAULT;
1411#endif
1412
1413/** @brief Fuel types for use in FUEL_TYPE. Fuel types specify the units for the maximum, available and consumed fuel, and for the flow rates. */
1414#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FUEL_TYPE
1415#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_FUEL_TYPE
1416typedef enum MAV_FUEL_TYPE
1417{
1418 MAV_FUEL_TYPE_UNKNOWN=0, /* Not specified. Fuel levels are normalized (i.e. maximum is 1, and other levels are relative to 1). | */
1419 MAV_FUEL_TYPE_LIQUID=1, /* A generic liquid fuel. Fuel levels are in millilitres (ml). Fuel rates are in millilitres/second. | */
1420 MAV_FUEL_TYPE_GAS=2, /* A gas tank. Fuel levels are in kilo-Pascal (kPa), and flow rates are in milliliters per second (ml/s). | */
1421 MAV_FUEL_TYPE_ENUM_END=3, /* | */
1422} MAV_FUEL_TYPE;
1423#endif
1424
1425/** @brief Flags to report status/failure cases for a power generator (used in GENERATOR_STATUS). Note that FAULTS are conditions that cause the generator to fail. Warnings are conditions that require attention before the next use (they indicate the system is not operating properly). */
1426#ifndef HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG
1427#define HAVE_ENUM_MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG
1428typedef enum MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG
1429{
1430 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_OFF=1, /* Generator is off. | */
1431 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_READY=2, /* Generator is ready to start generating power. | */
1432 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_GENERATING=4, /* Generator is generating power. | */
1433 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_CHARGING=8, /* Generator is charging the batteries (generating enough power to charge and provide the load). | */
1434 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_REDUCED_POWER=16, /* Generator is operating at a reduced maximum power. | */
1435 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_MAXPOWER=32, /* Generator is providing the maximum output. | */
1436 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_OVERTEMP_WARNING=64, /* Generator is near the maximum operating temperature, cooling is insufficient. | */
1437 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_OVERTEMP_FAULT=128, /* Generator hit the maximum operating temperature and shutdown. | */
1438 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_ELECTRONICS_OVERTEMP_WARNING=256, /* Power electronics are near the maximum operating temperature, cooling is insufficient. | */
1439 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_ELECTRONICS_OVERTEMP_FAULT=512, /* Power electronics hit the maximum operating temperature and shutdown. | */
1440 MAV_GENERATOR_STATUS_FLAG_ELECTRONICS_FAULT=1024, /* Power electronics experienced a fault and shutdown. | */
